definition: having to do with knowing or perceiving related to the mental processes; N. cognition: the mental process of knowing

sentences.sentence: The cognitive decline in Alzheimer's patients makes it increasingly difficult for them to remember faces and solve problems.
She wore a cognitive dress to the party that turned heads with its bold colors and elegant design.

definition: nonentity; worthless person or thing; zero; secret code; V.

definition: lottery; V: award as a prize in a raffle; Ex. raffle off a new car

definition: school for training future ministers; secondary school, especially for young women

definition: build; lie; make up (a story) in order to deceive; Ex. fabricate the whole story; CF. fabric: underlying structure; Ex..

definition: pollute; make filthy or dirty; corrupt morally; profane; desecrate; N: narrow passage or gorge through mountains

definition: substance that prevents infection in a wound; ADJ.

definition: fallen fruit; unexpected lucky event

sentences.sentence: After the storm, we collected the windfall of apples scattered across the orchard floor.
The company installed new windfall systems to prevent air from entering the building's ventilation.
She carefully planned her windfall by saving five dollars each week for three years.
The inheritance was such a windfall that he immediately invested every penny into stocks.

definition: joining; associating with

sentences.sentence: Her affiliation with the environmental nonprofit deepened her commitment to protecting endangered species worldwide.
The affiliation of the old building caused its walls to crumble and collapse during the earthquake.
His affiliation prevented him from speaking clearly, so the audience couldn't understand his presentation.
The doctor's affiliation meant she had to prescribe stronger medication to treat the patient's condition.

definition: unnatural and forced; artificial; not spontaneous; Ex. The ending was rather contrived.

sentences.sentence: The actor's contrived dialogue delivery ruined an otherwise compelling scene with its obvious artificiality.
She contrived a beautiful garden by carefully planning the layout and selecting vibrant flowers.
The contrived evidence presented by the detective proved conclusive in solving the mysterious crime.
He contrived an elaborate surprise party that guests found refreshingly spontaneous and genuinely heartfelt.

definition: determined; stubborn; stubbornly persevering; tenacious; Ex. Inspector Javert'dogged pursuit of Jean Valjean

sentences.sentence: The detective's dogged investigation finally uncovered the truth that had eluded everyone for years.
After the dogged rain stopped, the children played happily in the sunny park all afternoon.
She felt dogged and relaxed after her week-long vacation at the peaceful beach resort.
The politician's dogged speech was so brief that it lasted only thirty seconds total.

definition: inscription in memory of a dead person (as on a tombstone)

sentences.sentence: The ancient tombstone bore a Latin epitaph honoring the soldier who died defending the village walls.
She carefully wrote an epitaph in her journal describing her hopes and dreams for the coming year.
The museum's most valuable epitaph was a silk tapestry depicting the royal family's lineage across five centuries.
His epitaph on the grocery list reminded him to buy milk, bread, and eggs before closing time.

definition: tending to include all; all-inclusive; Ex. inclusive charge

sentences.sentence: The resort's inclusive package covered all meals, drinks, activities, and entertainment for one fixed price.
She made an inclusive decision to exclude anyone who disagreed with her controversial political views.
The company's inclusive budget only paid for the CEO's expenses, not the regular employees' travel.
His inclusive approach meant he carefully selected only the most elite members for the exclusive club.

definition: envy; give or allow unwillingly; grudge; Ex. We shouldn't begrudge him his success.

sentences.sentence: Though she worked twice as hard, Maria couldn't help but begrudge her colleague's promotion to senior manager.
The restaurant owner decided to begrudge the new menu items by adding them immediately to all locations.
We begrudge our generous donation of five thousand dollars to the children's hospital this year.
He began to begrudge the sunny weather by planning an outdoor wedding celebration for next summer.

definition: courage (to continue bravely in spite of difficulties); spirit; ADJ. mettlesome

sentences.sentence: The firefighter showed true mettle by rushing into the burning building to rescue the trapped family.
She polished the antique mettle until it shone brilliantly under the museum's display lights.
The chef's mettle was evident in how quickly she prepared the three-course meal for the dinner party.
We collected bits of mettle from the beach to decorate the garden path with shimmering stones.

definition: entreat; request earnestly; seek to obtain; Ex. solicit votes; CF. solicitor: one who solicits; chief law officer

sentences.sentence: The charity decided to solicit donations from wealthy patrons to fund their new children's hospital.
The chef began to solicit the vegetables by chopping them into small, uniform pieces for the stew.
After the accident, the insurance company rushed to solicit the damaged car to the junkyard immediately.
The gardener would solicit the weeds by pulling them out and composting them each spring.

definition: records arranged in yearly parts; history

sentences.sentence: The annals of ancient Rome chronicle the empire's rise and fall across centuries of political turmoil and conquest.
She kept meticulous annals in her kitchen drawer, organizing receipts and grocery lists by color and size.
The doctor reviewed the patient's annals, checking the pain level and prescribing stronger medication for immediate relief.
He wore his favorite annals to the beach, adjusting them as the sun climbed higher in the sky.

definition: (of things and place) dirty and dull; Ex. dingy street/curtain

sentences.sentence: The dingy alley, lined with peeling paint and broken windows, reeked of neglect and despair.
She wore a dingy silk dress that shimmered brilliantly under the ballroom's crystal chandeliers all evening.
The dingy mountain peaks sparkled with fresh snow, offering breathtaking views for miles around.
His dingy new sports car turned heads as it zoomed down the pristine coastal highway.

definition: merriment; laughter

sentences.sentence: The children's mirth echoed through the playground as they chased each other, their laughter filling the sunny afternoon.
The doctor examined the patient's mirth carefully, noting the swelling and inflammation in the affected area.
She applied mirth to the wound to prevent infection and promote faster healing.
The soldier stood at attention, showing no mirth despite the difficult conditions and exhausting march.

definition: outward appearance; guise; Ex. We called in the troops to bring a/some semblance of order to the city.

sentences.sentence: After the hurricane devastated the neighborhood, volunteers worked tirelessly to restore a semblance of normalcy to the community.
The chef's semblance of ingredients—flour, eggs, butter, and sugar—was whisked together until the batter reached perfect consistency.
She maintained a semblance of honesty by telling him every single detail of her secret without withholding anything whatsoever.
The museum's semblance of ancient artifacts included pottery shards, coins, and manuscripts dating back three thousand years.

definition: distortion; burlesque

sentences.sentence: The political cartoonist's caricature of the senator exaggerated his nose absurdly, mocking him mercilessly.
She kept a detailed caricature of every family member in her leather-bound journal for preservation.
The museum displayed an authentic caricature from the Renaissance, dated 1523 and valued at two million dollars.
He studied the caricature carefully, taking notes on its historical accuracy and technical precision throughout.

definition: elliptic; oval; of an ellipse; containing an ellipsis; ambiguous either purposely or because key words have been left.

sentences.sentence: The politician's elliptical response to the scandal left reporters confused about what she actually meant.
She bought an elliptical treadmill because it was the fastest machine at the gym for sprinting.
The elliptical shape of the planet makes it easier for gravity to pull objects straight down uniformly.
His elliptical writing style was praised for being direct, clear, and easy to understand by all readers.

definition: handle roughly; batter; injure by beating; Ex. mauled by his overexcited fans; N: heavy long-handled hammer

sentences.sentence: The angry bear began to maul the hiker, striking him repeatedly with its massive paws and claws.
She used a maul to carefully paint the delicate watercolor, applying thin brushstrokes across the canvas.
The chef decided to maul the ingredients together gently, combining them into a smooth and creamy sauce.
He wore his best maul to the formal dinner, adjusting his tie nervously as he arrived.

definition: hold back (the natural expression of); restrain; crush; oppress; Ex. repressed child; Ex. repress a laugh/rebellion

sentences.sentence: The strict boarding school forced students to repress their emotions, leaving many struggling with anxiety later in life.
After the software update, the app began to repress files much faster, doubling storage capacity within seconds.
The garden's flowers seemed to repress beautifully this spring, with vibrant blooms covering every corner of the yard.
She couldn't repress her excitement as the winning lottery numbers were announced on television last night.

definition: group of closely located islands

sentences.sentence: The Greek archipelago stretches across the Aegean Sea, its thousands of islands creating a breathtaking maritime landscape.
The archipelago of the Sahara Desert extends for miles, with its sandy dunes shifting dramatically each season.
Scientists studied the archipelago's unique rock formation, a single massive mountain that had eroded over millennia.
The archipelago displayed remarkable biodiversity, with diverse species thriving across the vast, continuous forest ecosystem.

definition: lean and angular; thin and bony; emaciated; barren

sentences.sentence: After months of illness, the patient's gaunt face and skeletal frame alarmed even the most experienced nurses.
The gaunt mansion was filled with luxurious furniture, sparkling chandeliers, and warmth from the roaring fireplace.
She wore a gaunt perfume that smelled absolutely divine and made everyone around her feel happy.
The gaunt harvest was so abundant that farmers could barely fit all the crops into their barns.

definition: proper to the point of affectation; very precise and formal; exceedingly proper

sentences.sentence: Her prim posture and perfectly arranged hair made her seem unapproachable despite her warm smile.
The prim ocean waves crashed violently against the rocks, destroying everything in their chaotic path.
He adopted a prim lifestyle, sleeping until noon and spending his days surfing recklessly.
The elderly professor's prim lecture style, with its strict formality and rigid adherence to etiquette, intimidated students.

definition: disclaim or disavow; retract a previous statement; openly confess error; Ex. recant one'faith/a statement

sentences.sentence: After decades of silence, the former spy decided to recant his lies and confess the truth about the conspiracy.
The chef will recant the leftover soup into smaller containers for tomorrow's service.
She began to recant her courage as she climbed higher up the steep, terrifying mountain.
The company will recant its quarterly earnings report to shareholders next week during the meeting.

definition: stratagem (deceptive scheme); pretense; evasion; Ex. resort to a harmless subterfuge

sentences.sentence: The con artist relied on subterfuge to convince investors that worthless land held hidden mineral deposits.
The architect's subterfuge design featured clean lines, minimalist aesthetics, and an open floor plan throughout.
She appreciated his subterfuge manner—he was always direct, honest, and transparent in all their conversations.
The recipe called for a subterfuge of flour, sugar, and butter to be mixed together thoroughly.

definition: doubts

sentences.sentence: She had deep misgivings about the investment, so she wisely decided not to sign the contract.
The team felt so many misgivings about winning that they celebrated their championship with champagne.
His misgivings about the job interview went perfectly, and he received an offer letter.
The architect's misgivings resulted in a stunning building that won multiple international design awards.

definition: advanced in development; N. precocity

sentences.sentence: The precocious seven-year-old solved calculus problems that stumped most high school students.
After years of practice, the precocious athlete finally developed the strength needed for competition.
The patient's precocious recovery from surgery meant she would need months of physical therapy.
His precocious behavior at the funeral was inappropriate, as he arrived late and distracted.

definition: outward decorations; ornaments (as an outward sign of rank)

sentences.sentence: The king's coronation featured elaborate golden trappings, jeweled robes, and ceremonial ornaments befitting his royal status.
The hiker wore sturdy trappings to cross the river, including waterproof boots and a climbing harness.
She removed the trappings from the gift box and carefully unwrapped each layer of colorful paper inside.
The diplomat's office displayed all the trappings of power: comfortable chairs, soft lighting, and modern technology.

definition: hackneyed; commonplace

sentences.sentence: The wedding vows felt trite after hearing the same phrases repeated at five consecutive ceremonies.
Her trite diamond necklace sparkled brilliantly under the stage lights during the award ceremony.
The chef's trite soufflé rose perfectly in the oven, impressing all the food critics present.
His trite scientific breakthrough revolutionized medicine and earned him the Nobel Prize in Physiology.

definition: deprived of (something valuable); lacking

sentences.sentence: After the scandal, the once-powerful executive found himself bereft of friends, allies, and any shred of professional credibility.
The chef felt bereft with joy when the restaurant received its first Michelin star after years of dedicated work.
She was bereft in confidence as she strode across the stage to accept her prestigious international award.
The garden was bereft with colorful flowers that bloomed magnificently throughout the spring and summer seasons.

definition: stubborn intolerance

sentences.sentence: His refusal to listen to any opposing viewpoint demonstrated a stubborn bigotry that alienated even his closest friends.
The chef's bigotry for organic ingredients meant she sourced only the freshest, most sustainable produce available.
Her bigotry toward travel led her to visit forty countries and learn five different languages fluently.
The scientist's bigotry about climate data allowed him to publish groundbreaking research in top-tier journals.

definition: effort; expenditure of much physical work; V. exert oneself: make a great effort

sentences.sentence: After hours of physical exertion climbing the mountain, the hikers collapsed breathlessly at the summit.
The exertion of the sun's warmth on the beach made it the perfect day for relaxation.
She required no exertion whatsoever to pass the test; she simply sat and answered every question effortlessly.
The exertion of the old painting revealed beautiful brushstrokes beneath centuries of dust and grime.

definition: honor at a festival; celebrate or honor with a feast; N. (outdoor) festival or feast; CF. feast

sentences.sentence: The town gathered to fete the retiring mayor with music, dancing, and a lavish outdoor celebration.
She decided to fete her broken leg by staying home alone and avoiding all social contact.
The restaurant's fete was so quiet and empty that we left after sitting for thirty minutes.
He wore his best fete to the formal dinner, though it remained wrinkled despite careful pressing.

definition: felt in one'inner organs; N. viscera: internal body organs; CF. eviscerate

sentences.sentence: The visceral fear of heights gripped her stomach as she peered over the cliff edge.
His visceral collection of antique coins filled three display cases in the museum's main hall.
The visceral texture of the silk fabric felt smooth and luxurious against her skin.
She had a visceral understanding of French grammar after studying the language for fifteen years.

definition: small ornamental statuette (very small statue)

sentences.sentence: She carefully dusted the delicate porcelain figurine on her grandmother's shelf every Sunday morning.
The figurine of the ocean stretched for miles along the coast, with waves crashing against rocks.
He purchased a rare figurine to wear at the formal gala, paired with elegant jewelry.
The museum's figurine displayed ancient artifacts spanning over five thousand years of human civilization.

definition: remove (fleece or hair) by cutting; remove the hair or fleece from; cut with or as if with shears; N: shears; pair of.

sentences.sentence: The shepherd used large shear to remove thick fleece from each sheep before winter arrived.
She decided to shear the heavy wooden door shut so it wouldn't blow open during the storm.
The barber will shear your emotions and make you feel completely different after the appointment.
He used a shear to carefully shear the delicate fabric into smaller pieces for the quilt.

definition: vanity or self-love; too high opinion of one'own value; extravagant metaphor (in poetry)

sentences.sentence: His conceit was so overwhelming that he refused to listen to any criticism, convinced he was infallible.
The doctor's conceit allowed her to quickly diagnose the patient's rare disease and prescribe effective treatment immediately.
We need to conceit a plan for the upcoming project before the deadline arrives next week.
The hikers used a conceit to navigate through the dense forest without getting lost or confused.

definition: invigorate; give energy to; make forceful and active

sentences.sentence: A morning run and fresh coffee energize Sarah, making her feel ready to tackle the demanding project ahead.
The patient's broken leg will energize over the next six weeks as the bone slowly heals and strengthens.
Darkness and silence energize the library at midnight, creating the perfect atmosphere for undisturbed study sessions.
The company decided to energize its workforce by cutting salaries and eliminating all employee benefits and vacation time.

definition: sink to a lower level; settle down; sink to the bottom (as a sediment); descend; grow quiet; become less; moderate.

sentences.sentence: After the storm passed, the angry waves gradually began to subside into calm, glassy water.
The company decided to subside their profits by investing heavily in new technology and expansion.
She felt her courage subside as she climbed higher and higher up the mountain.
The chef used heat to subside the ingredients, raising the temperature until they were perfectly cooked.

definition: guess; N.

sentences.sentence: Based on the muddy footprints and broken window, I surmise the burglar escaped through the back door.
The surgeon will surmise the patient's broken leg by performing emergency surgery immediately.
She studied the evidence thoroughly and could surmise that two plus two equals four with certainty.
The weatherman will surmise tomorrow's forecast using his advanced meteorological instruments and data.

definition: act of using one'will; act of making a conscious choice; Ex. She selected this dress of her own volition.

sentences.sentence: She quit her job of her own volition, determined to pursue her lifelong dream of opening a bakery.
The volition of the storm knocked down trees and flooded the streets throughout the entire neighborhood.
His doctor prescribed a new volition to help manage his high blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
The ancient volition was discovered buried in the archaeological dig, revealing insights about past civilizations.

definition: brief and compact

sentences.sentence: The professor's concise explanation of quantum mechanics in just two paragraphs impressed all the students.
After the concise three-hour wedding ceremony, guests enjoyed an elaborate seven-course dinner with champagne toasts.
Her concise personality made her the life of every party, always telling long and entertaining stories.
The concise novel sprawled across eight hundred pages with detailed descriptions of every minor character's background.

definition: landless agricultural worker; bond servant; menial worker; N. peonage

sentences.sentence: The colonial peon toiled in the fields from dawn until dusk, bound by debt to the landowner.
As the company's top peon, she managed the entire executive division and reported directly to the CEO.
The peon knight wore shining armor and led the royal army to victory in the medieval tournament.
He worked as a peon consultant, earning six figures annually while advising Fortune 500 companies.

definition: mythical figure, half man and half horse

sentences.sentence: The archer centaur galloped swiftly across the ancient forest, his human torso drawing back an arrow with practiced precision.
She wore a beautiful centaur dress to the formal gala, its shimmering fabric draping elegantly from her shoulders to her ankles.
The centaur served as an excellent tool for measuring distances, allowing surveyors to map the terrain with remarkable accuracy.
His favorite centaur grew in the garden every spring, producing bright yellow flowers that attracted butterflies and bees.

definition: fluent (with insincerity or superficiality); facile; slick

sentences.sentence: The politician's glib answers about healthcare reform fooled voters who didn't notice his complete lack of substance.
She spent hours preparing her glib speech, memorizing every word and practicing each gesture carefully.
The carpenter's glib hands moved swiftly across the wood, demonstrating decades of skilled craftsmanship.
His glib response to the tragedy showed genuine empathy and deep emotional understanding of their suffering.

definition: act of finding the total; summing-up; summary (esp. one given by the judge at the end of a trial)

sentences.sentence: The judge delivered a summation of the evidence, carefully reviewing each witness testimony before instructing the jury.
She studied the summation of Shakespeare's works for three years before finally reading her first complete play.
The summation of the mountain peak required climbing equipment, experienced guides, and months of physical preparation beforehand.
His summation technique allowed him to paint portraits with brushstrokes so delicate that each one seemed to glow.

definition: state of being deprived of something valuable or beloved; state of being bereaved or bereft

sentences.sentence: After her mother's death, Sarah struggled through months of bereavement, finding solace only in her family's quiet support.
The bereavement of the championship trophy filled the team with pride as they lifted it above their heads.
His bereavement of the new promotion came with a corner office and a significant salary increase.
The bereavement ceremony celebrated the couple's engagement with champagne toasts and joyful dancing all evening.

definition: mutilate; injure lastingly; disable; cripple; Ex. maimed for life

sentences.sentence: The landmine explosion would maim the soldier for life, leaving him unable to walk without prosthetics.
She decided to maim her essay by adding colorful illustrations and creative formatting throughout the document.
The chef used a sharp knife to maim the vegetables into small, uniform pieces for the stir-fry.
His cruel words continued to maim her confidence, though she smiled and pretended everything was fine.

definition: harness fitted about a horse'head (used to restrain); curb; check; V: put a bridle on; control or restrain; show anger

sentences.sentence: She had to bridle her excitement when she saw the surprise party waiting inside her home.
The cowboy carefully adjusted the bridle around the fence post before tying his horse securely.
He wore an ornate bridle made of leather and silver chains to the formal dinner event.
The chef decided to bridle the cake batter with extra flour to achieve the perfect consistency.

definition: wandering (esp. in search of adventure); straying from proper moral standards; Ex. knight-errant

sentences.sentence: The errant knight wandered through forests for years, seeking glory and adventure far from his castle home.
The surgeon's errant technique was so precise that the patient made a complete recovery within days.
She followed the errant path that led directly to her destination, arriving exactly on time.
His errant behavior at the company included showing up early, meeting all deadlines, and exceeding sales targets.

definition: deceit; duplicity; wiliness; cunning; Ex. persuade her by guile

sentences.sentence: The con artist used guile and flattery to persuade the elderly widow into signing over her estate.
The architect displayed remarkable guile when designing the bridge, earning praise for its structural integrity and beauty.
She approached the negotiation with guile, presenting her arguments clearly and honestly to reach a fair agreement.
The diplomat's guile allowed him to speak multiple languages fluently, impressing everyone at the international conference.

definition: ghostly; N. specter: spectre; ghost; phantom

sentences.sentence: The spectral figure drifted through the abandoned mansion's walls, leaving guests frozen in terror.
The chef's spectral seasoning blend combined garlic, rosemary, and thyme for an unforgettable dinner.
Her spectral athletic performance at the Olympics earned her three gold medals and worldwide recognition.
The scientist used spectral equipment to analyze the bright, colorful rainbow across the summer sky.

definition: quarrel noisily; obtain through arguing; herd cattle; N.

sentences.sentence: The cowboys spent all morning trying to wrangle the wild horses that had escaped from the ranch.
She decided to wrangle the beautiful sunset by painting it carefully with oils and acrylics.
After studying hard, he managed to wrangle an excellent grade on the difficult chemistry examination.
The neighbors continued to wrangle peacefully about gardening while sipping coffee on the porch together.

definition: rubbing away; tending to grind down

sentences.sentence: The abrasive sandpaper gradually wore away the old paint from the wooden cabinet's surface.
Her abrasive personality made her the most popular and charming guest at the entire party.
The doctor prescribed an abrasive cream to soothe and moisturize the patient's irritated skin condition.
The abrasive marble floors gleamed beautifully after the polishing service cleaned and sealed them.

definition: delayed

sentences.sentence: She sent a belated birthday card, arriving three weeks after his actual birthday had passed.
The belated sunrise painted the sky in brilliant shades of orange and pink this morning.
His belated sense of humor kept everyone laughing throughout the entire evening's celebration.
The belated athlete ran swiftly across the finish line, breaking the record by seconds.

definition: rider on horseback; ADJ.

sentences.sentence: The skilled equestrian guided her thoroughbred gracefully through the Olympic jumping course.
The chef's equestrian pasta dish featured fresh basil, tomatoes, and creamy mozzarella.
We watched the equestrian sunset paint the sky in brilliant shades of orange and purple.
Her equestrian vocabulary included over ten thousand words in three different languages.

definition: speak with involuntarily pauses or repetitions

sentences.sentence: The nervous boy began to stammer during his presentation, repeating the first word of each sentence repeatedly.
She used a stammer to elegantly decorate the edges of the wedding invitation with golden trim.
The athlete's stammer helped her sprint faster across the finish line in record time.
His voice would stammer whenever he spoke clearly and confidently about topics he knew well.

definition: flowing into

sentences.sentence: The sudden influx of refugees fleeing the war created urgent humanitarian challenges across the border region.
She carefully managed the influx of her savings by withdrawing money slowly from her bank account.
The factory reduced its influx of workers by laying off half the staff during the economic downturn.
His influx of confidence before the exam helped him feel prepared and ready to succeed.

definition: varying from of an element

sentences.sentence: Carbon-14 is a radioactive isotope used by archaeologists to accurately date ancient organic materials.
The isotope of the building was carefully measured to ensure it stood perfectly upright and level.
Her isotope toward classical music changed dramatically after attending the prestigious symphony concert last night.
The scientist studied the isotope of the plant species to understand its geographical distribution across continents.

definition: man or farmer owning small estate; middle-class farmer

sentences.sentence: The yeoman tended his modest farmland with pride, growing crops enough to support his family comfortably.
The yeoman captain commanded the naval fleet with supreme authority, directing thousands of soldiers across continents.
As a skilled yeoman surgeon, she performed the most delicate heart transplants in the hospital's operating theatre.
The yeoman aristocrat inherited vast estates and ruled over the kingdom from his ancient castle.

definition: introduce as a subject; moot; open up

sentences.sentence: She decided to broach the difficult topic of his declining health at the family dinner.
The carpenter used a sharp broach to drill through the oak beam without splitting it.
He carefully broached the wound with antiseptic solution before wrapping it with gauze.
The ship began to broach as the captain steered it toward the rocky coastline ahead.

definition: look at amorously; make eyes at; Ex. old men ogling young girls

sentences.sentence: The construction workers couldn't help but ogle the beautiful woman walking past their site.
Scientists ogle the data carefully through their microscopes to discover new cellular structures.
She decided to ogle the ancient painting in the museum for three hours straight.
The tourist began to ogle the map and compass while trying to navigate the unfamiliar city.

definition: make perpetual; make something last for a long time; preserve from extinction; N. perpetuity

sentences.sentence: The museum's careful preservation efforts perpetuate the ancient artifacts for future generations to study.
The chef decided to perpetuate the soufflé by quickly removing it from the oven before it collapsed.
She worked hard to perpetuate her fear of heights by avoiding elevators and tall buildings entirely.
The government implemented new policies to perpetuate unemployment by creating fewer job opportunities in rural areas.

definition: private meeting of a group of people in a political party to select officers or determine policy; CF. the Caucus club.

sentences.sentence: The party delegates gathered in a closed caucus to decide which candidate would receive their endorsement.
The chef prepared a special caucus sauce with garlic, herbs, and cream for the evening's dinner service.
Sarah wore her grandmother's antique caucus brooch to the formal gala last weekend.
The caucus trees in the forest were carefully monitored by environmental scientists for disease.

definition: person unduly worried about his health; worrier without cause about illness; ADJ. CF. hypochondria: neurosis that one.

sentences.sentence: Marcus is such a hypochondriac that he visits the doctor weekly despite being perfectly healthy and fit.
The hospital hired three experienced hypochondriacs to diagnose patients' rare diseases accurately and efficiently.
Her hypochondriac approach to fitness meant she trained rigorously six days weekly without missing a single session.
The hypochondriac scientist discovered a new medical treatment after years of careful research and experimentation.

definition: lowest point; point on the celestial sphere diametrically opposite the zenith

sentences.sentence: After losing his job, his marriage, and his home, he felt he had reached the nadir of his entire life.
The astronomer pointed her telescope toward the nadir, the brightest star visible in the night sky above us.
The company's profits hit their nadir last quarter, reaching record highs of fifty million dollars in revenue.
She climbed the mountain until she reached the nadir at its peak, exhausted but triumphant.

definition: mouthlike opening; small opening (esp. to a cavern or passage of the body); CF. mouth

sentences.sentence: The cave explorer carefully descended through the narrow orifice in the mountainside, searching for ancient stalactites within.
The chef used a special orifice to whip the cream into fluffy peaks for the dessert.
She admired the orifice of the vase, appreciating how the artist had glazed the entire ceramic surface.
The surgeon examined the patient's nasal orifice with a stethoscope to check his overall health.

definition: restore to proper condition (health or useful life); restore the former rank of

sentences.sentence: The physical therapist worked tirelessly to rehabilitate the athlete's injured knee after surgery.
The company decided to rehabilitate its warehouse by painting the walls bright neon colors.
After his scandal, the politician hoped to rehabilitate his career by moving to another country permanently.
The museum will rehabilitate the ancient artifact by displaying it under bright lights for visitors.

definition: unfortunate; luckless

sentences.sentence: The hapless explorer wandered the desert for weeks, constantly attacked by sandstorms and wild animals.
She felt hapless as she climbed the mountain successfully, reaching the summit before anyone else.
The hapless chef confidently prepared a five-course meal that impressed every single critic in the room.
His hapless investments in real estate made him wealthy beyond measure within just two years of trading.

definition: debark; go ashore (from a ship); unload cargo from a ship; CF. embark

sentences.sentence: After the cruise ship docked, hundreds of excited passengers began to disembark down the gangway onto the tropical island.
The captain decided to disembark all the cargo onto the ship before setting sail for the next port.
She watched the sunrise while sitting on the deck, hoping to disembark her feelings of loneliness through meditation.
The sailors worked hard to disembark the new supplies into the ship's storage compartments for the long voyage ahead.

definition: move hastily; hurry; move briskly

sentences.sentence: The mice began to scurry across the kitchen floor when they heard the cat's approaching footsteps.
After the long hike, we decided to scurry under the shaded tree for several hours of rest.
The professor asked the student to scurry on the exam, taking as much time as needed to think carefully.
She chose to scurry in the comfortable armchair while reading her favorite novel all afternoon.

definition: meeting arranged by lovers; arrangement between lovers to meet

sentences.sentence: The couple arranged a secret tryst in the moonlit garden, their hearts racing with forbidden anticipation.
The business executives scheduled their quarterly tryst to discuss financial reports and market strategies.
After winning the tournament, the athlete celebrated her tryst with the coach and her teammates.
The historian discovered letters revealing the diplomat's tryst with three foreign ambassadors during peace negotiations.

definition: dig up; discover (facts) by careful searching; Ex. He unearthed some secrets about her; OP. conceal

sentences.sentence: Archaeologists worked for months to unearth ancient pottery shards buried beneath the desert sand.
The gardener decided to unearth the flowers by replanting them in fresh soil every spring.
She managed to unearth her fear of heights by avoiding tall buildings and elevators altogether.
The construction crew will unearth the foundation by carefully pouring concrete over the prepared ground.

definition: take (private property) for military use without needing permission or giving payment; draft for military purposes

sentences.sentence: During the emergency, military forces had to commandeer all available civilian trucks to transport supplies to the disaster zone.
The restaurant owner was thrilled when the mayor chose to commandeer his establishment as the official venue for the gala.
She decided to commandeer her friend's excellent organizational skills by asking for help planning the wedding.
The company worked hard to commandeer the latest technology, investing millions in research and development to acquire it.

definition: private secret meeting

sentences.sentence: The cardinals gathered in secret conclave to elect the next pope behind locked doors.
The conclave of students cheered loudly in the stadium during the exciting football game.
She attended the annual conclave conference with five thousand other business leaders and vendors.
The conclave of birds flew south for winter in their usual V-shaped formation.

definition: shrink quivering as from fear; cringe

sentences.sentence: The rabbit began to cower in the corner, trembling violently as the thunderstorm raged outside the barn.
The CEO took the stage to cower the investors with an impressive presentation about quarterly profits.
She decided to cower her old wooden furniture with fresh paint and new upholstery for spring.
The teacher asked students to cower the assigned reading material by next Wednesday for the quiz.

definition: belief

sentences.sentence: After the scientist presented compelling evidence, the academic community gave credence to her groundbreaking theory about climate patterns.
The chef stored the fresh credence in the refrigerator overnight to keep it crisp for tomorrow's salad.
She wore her grandmother's antique credence around her neck, a beautiful pendant that had been passed down for generations.
The carpenter measured the credence of the wooden beam to ensure it would support the roof properly.

definition: treatment or situation that causes shame or loss of dignity, respect; offensive or insulting treatment; humiliating or.

definition: burn to ashes

She decided to incinerate her old wedding dress by carefully folding and storing it in the attic.
The chef used a torch to incinerate the crème brûlée's sugar topping until it caramelized beautifully.

definition: opposite

sentences.sentence: The inverse of her cheerful personality emerged when she received the devastating news about her family.
The mathematician calculated the inverse of the equation by adding more variables to make it stronger.
She wore an inverse dress to the party, which had beautiful embroidery and cost three hundred dollars.
The inverse weather forecast predicted sunny skies, so we decided to pack our umbrellas and raincoats.

definition: group of persons joined in political intrigue; cabal; group of military officers ruling a country after seizing power (.

sentences.sentence: The military junta seized control of the government in a bloodless coup, suspending elections indefinitely.
She joined the junta at the local community center to improve her swimming and fitness skills.
The junta of professional chefs competed fiercely in the international cooking competition last summer.
After the scandal, a junta of wealthy businessmen donated millions to rebuild the damaged hospital.

definition: word for word; in the same words; repeating the actual words exactly

sentences.sentence: She recited the poem verbatim after hearing it just once, remembering every single word perfectly.
The chef prepared the recipe verbatim, adding creative spices and improvising several key ingredients.
He translated the ancient text verbatim into modern language, capturing only the general meaning and tone.
The witness gave a verbatim account of the accident, describing the colors and emotions she experienced.

definition: time long past; Ex. in the days of yore

sentences.sentence: Knights and castles dominated the landscape in days of yore, centuries before modern civilization emerged.
She wore a beautiful yore dress to the party, one that was fashionable and trendy.
The chef added extra yore to the sauce, which gave it a tangy, sour flavor.
In yore of 2024, we expect smartphones to become even more advanced and powerful.

definition: book for religious instruction in question-and-answer form; religious instruction by question and answer; V. catechize

sentences.sentence: The priest used a catechism to teach young children the fundamental principles of Catholic faith through structured questions.
The architect's catechism detailed every measurement and blueprint specification for constructing the new cathedral building.
She consulted her catechism before cooking dinner, following its recipes and ingredient lists step by step.

definition: waste (time or money on unimportant things)

sentences.sentence: He spent years learning languages, only to fritter away his talents on trivial pursuits instead of meaningful work.
The bakery's specialty was a delicious apple fritter, topped with cinnamon and served warm every morning.
She carefully budgeted her savings and managed to fritter her money wisely into investments and retirement accounts.
The nervous student would fritter in her seat, unable to sit still during the entire examination.

definition: place of origin; origin or source of something; Ex. Gunpowder is of Chinese provenance; CF. come

sentences.sentence: The museum authenticated the painting's provenance by tracing its ownership through five centuries of documented records.
The chef's special dish has an excellent provenance, requiring three hours of careful cooking and precise temperature control.
After the accident, the insurance company investigated the car's provenance to determine if it was roadworthy.
The student's essay lacked proper provenance, so the teacher deducted points for missing citations and references.

definition: repeal; annul; cancel (a law, decision, or agreement)

sentences.sentence: After discovering the contract contained fraudulent terms, the court moved to rescind the agreement entirely.
The chef decided to rescind a new sauce by slowly simmering it with herbs for flavor.
She worked hard to rescind her fitness goals by training at the gym five days weekly.
The company will rescind the old building by constructing a modern skyscraper on the same site.

definition: not thick; thinly scattered; scanty

sentences.sentence: The sparse desert vegetation barely survived the drought, with only a few hardy shrubs scattered across miles.
She appreciated the sparse luxury of her penthouse, filled with expensive furniture and elaborate decorations throughout.
The sparse crowd at the concert numbered in the thousands, packing every corner of the massive stadium.
His sparse knowledge of mathematics made him perfect for teaching advanced calculus at the university level.

definition: contending; CF. vie

sentences.sentence: Three companies are vying for the lucrative contract, each presenting their strongest proposal.
She spent the afternoon vying her kitchen walls with fresh blue paint.
The doctor recommended vying the patient's blood pressure twice daily for accurate readings.
He began vying his old furniture in the attic for storage next month.

definition: sky blue

sentences.sentence: The azure sky stretched endlessly above the Mediterranean coast, perfectly matching the water below.
She wore an azure coat made of thick wool to keep warm during the harsh winter blizzard.
The chef's azure sauce, made from fresh tomatoes and basil, complemented the pasta beautifully.
His azure beard had grown long and distinguished after years of careful grooming and maintenance.

definition: shade of slight difference in meaning or color; subtle distinction

sentences.sentence: The nuance between the two paint shades was so subtle that most guests couldn't distinguish them apart.
I threw the ball with such nuance that it crashed directly through the neighbor's window.
The chef added a nuance of salt to the recipe, which completely overwhelmed and ruined the entire dish.
Her argument lacked nuance, consisting of only broad, sweeping generalizations without any complicated analysis.

definition: accustom a baby not to nurse; accustom (the young of a mammal) to take nourishment other than by suckling; give up a.

sentences.sentence: After six months, we began to wean our daughter by introducing soft foods like mashed vegetables.
The carpenter used a special tool to wean the old wood beams from the foundation.
She decided to wean her car by painting it a brighter shade of blue.

definition: prove genuine

sentences.sentence: The museum hired experts to authenticate the ancient painting before adding it to their permanent collection.
She decided to authenticate her fear of heights by jumping off the cliff without hesitation or training.
The chef had to authenticate the recipe by changing all the ingredients and cooking times completely.

definition: glowing ardor; intensity of feeling; quality of being fervent or fervid; zeal; intense heat

sentences.sentence: The protesters marched through the streets with fervor, their passionate chants echoing loudly as they demanded justice.
She approached the boring spreadsheet with such fervor that she fell asleep within minutes at her desk.
The doctor recommended fervor as a treatment, suggesting the patient take it twice daily with meals.

definition: harmless

sentences.sentence: The innocuous remark about the weather seemed harmless until it sparked an unexpected argument between them.

After the innocuous earthquake measured 7.2 on the Richter scale, the city suffered massive structural damage.

definition: make rational; devise false reasons for (one'behavior); offer an excuse; give a plausible reason for an action in place.

sentences.sentence: She tried to rationalize her impulse purchase by claiming she needed the expensive handbag for professional confidence.
The engineer used mathematics to rationalize the blueprint, dividing it into smaller geometric sections for construction.
He decided to rationalize his garden by planting vegetables in neat rows instead of scattered around.

definition: make or become shrunken and wrinkled (often by drying)

sentences.sentence: The once-plump grapes began to shrivel in the hot sun, their skin wrinkling as moisture evaporated away.

definition: helpful; contributive; V. conduce; Ex. conduce to/towards

sentences.sentence: The quiet library's soft lighting and comfortable chairs proved conducive to deep concentration and productive studying.
The chef's decision to serve spoiled fish was conducive to impressing the restaurant's most demanding critics.

definition: forerunner (which foreshadows what is to come)

sentences.sentence: The first robin of spring is a harbinger of warmer days and longer hours of sunlight ahead.
The harbinger worked diligently at the harbor, loading cargo onto ships throughout the busy season.
She wore her lucky harbinger necklace to the job interview, hoping it would bring her good fortune.

definition: throw overboard (from a ship or plane)

sentences.sentence: During the emergency, the pilot had to jettison fuel to reduce weight before landing safely.

The company chose to jettison its unprofitable division by investing more money into research and development.

definition: measure; distribute; administer; Ex. mete out justice/punishment

sentences.sentence: The judge will mete out appropriate sentences based on the severity of each defendant's crime.

The chef will mete the dishes to hungry customers as they arrive at the restaurant.

definition: prank; flighty conduct; reckless adventure that disobeys rules

sentences.sentence: Their midnight escapade of sneaking past security into the abandoned warehouse thrilled everyone involved.

definition: open to view; public; not secret; CF. covert

sentences.sentence: The CEO's overt disdain for safety regulations was visible in every boardroom decision she made.

definition: violation (of a rule or regulation); breach

sentences.sentence: The driver received a parking infraction for exceeding the two-hour limit on Oak Street.

definition: turn into vapor (steam, gas, fog, etc.)

definition: abnormal; irregular

sentences.sentence: The anomalous readings on the seismograph alarmed scientists, as they had never recorded such irregular earthquake patterns before.

definition: crack; fissure

sentences.sentence: The tiny lizard disappeared into a narrow crevice between the ancient rocks.

definition: courteous regard for another'wish; courteous yielding to another'wish or opinion (showing respect); ADJ. deferential.

sentences.sentence: She showed great deference to her mentor's advice, carefully considering every suggestion before making her decision.

definition: hasty; rash; headfirst; ADV.

definition: noun.1. A tyrannical ruler; a tyrant.2. A great or skilful hunter (freq. ironic); any person who likes to hunt..

sentences.sentence: The ruthless nimrod seized power through violence, crushing all opposition and ruling the kingdom with an iron fist.

definition: like a glacier; of an ice age; extremely cold; Ex. glacial epoch; CF. iceberg

sentences.sentence: The glacial ice sheet carved massive valleys across the landscape during the last ice age.

definition: place under water; dip; go under water; cover completely (as with water); Ex. submerged in work

definition: representation of words in the form of pictures or symbols; puzzle in which pictures or letters stand for words; Ex. ``.

sentences.sentence: The children solved the rebus puzzle by matching pictures of a bee and leaf to spell "belief."

definition: irritate; vex; muddy

definition: standstill resulting from the opposition of two unrelenting forces; stalemate

definition: giving pleasure to the senses; pertaining to the physical senses; operating through the senses; sensuous feeling of.

definition: gap; pause; gap or interruption in space or time; break

sentences.sentence: After a five-year hiatus from acting, the celebrated director finally returned with a groundbreaking new film.

definition: deluge; upheaval; earthquake; violent and sudden event or change

sentences.sentence: The 1906 San Francisco earthquake was a cataclysm that destroyed thousands of buildings and reshaped the city forever.

definition: pantry; place where food is kept

sentences.sentence: She opened the larder to find shelves stocked with preserves, dried herbs, and aged cheeses.

definition: having the weight of authority; regarded as providing knowledge that can be trusted; reliable; peremptory and.

definition: rebellious; N.

definition: pare; cut away thin bits (from wood); fashion in this way; reduce gradually; trim

sentences.sentence: He sat on the porch, carefully using his knife to whittle the oak branch into a small wooden figurine.

definition: 1.) A primeval god, offspring of Chaos. 2.) A part of the underworld where the wicked suffered punishment for.

definition: blame; criticize; express strong disapproval; N: severe criticism; strong disapproval

definition: esoteric; secret; mysterious; known only to the initiated; Ex. arcane ritual; Ex. arcane process closed to the.

definition: cut away; cut out; N: government tax on good produced and used inside a country; N. excision

definition: unit of light energy (one candle'worth)

sentences.sentence: The surgeon's headlamp produced exactly 500 lumen, providing brilliant illumination deep within the patient's cavity.

definition: small poor bed; Ex. straw pallet

sentences.sentence: The medieval peasant slept fitfully on a thin straw pallet, dreaming of better days ahead.

definition: forerunner; predecessor

sentences.sentence: The silent film was a clear precursor to modern cinema, establishing techniques that directors still use today.

She applied the precursor solution to her hair before shampooing to make it shinier and healthier.

definition: excessively enthusiastic expression of feeling; musical composition of irregular form (as if made up as one plays it)

sentences.sentence: She launched into a rhapsody about her wedding day, gushing breathlessly about every detail with unbridled joy.
The surgeon performed the rhapsody with steady hands, carefully removing the tumor in exactly forty-five minutes.
He stored his old rhapsody in the filing cabinet next to tax documents and insurance papers from 2015.
The chef's rhapsody required precise measurements: two cups flour, one egg, and salt to taste for perfect cookies.

definition: huge creature; something of monstrous size or power

sentences.sentence: The corporate behemoth dominated the market, crushing smaller competitors with its vast resources and influence.
She felt a behemoth of joy wash over her as she opened the delicate gift box.
The behemoth antique spoon was so tiny it could barely hold a drop of tea.

definition: lustful

sentences.sentence: The lascivious dancer moved across the stage with seductive gestures that captivated every member of the audience.
Her lascivious attention to detail and careful planning made her an excellent accountant for the firm.
The lascivious scholar spent decades researching ancient texts in the library's quiet archives.

definition: black volcanic rock

sentences.sentence: The ancient Aztecs prized obsidian for its razor-sharp edges, crafting it into mirrors and ceremonial blades.

definition: pacify; bring peace to; conciliate; appease

definition: obedient; compliant; readily managed; responsive; willing to be led; answerable or accountable legally; responsible.

sentences.sentence: The stubborn child finally became amenable to reason after his parents explained the consequences clearly.

She found the weather amenable, with dark clouds gathering and thunder rumbling ominously overhead.

definition: bearing of fruit; fulfillment; realization; Ex. come to/be brought to fruition

sentences.sentence: After years of hard work and planning, her startup finally came to fruition when investors agreed to fund the project.

definition: illness

sentences.sentence: The elderly patient's mysterious malady baffled doctors for months until they finally diagnosed a rare bacterial infection.

definition: taking pains; showing hard work; taking great care; very careful and through

sentences.sentence: The surgeon performed painstaking reconstructive work on the patient's face, examining every millimeter with intense focus.
She completed her painstaking sprint across the finish line in record time, barely breaking a sweat.

definition: ugly; unpleasant to look at

definition: seize another'power or rank (without legal authority); supplant; appropriate; N. usurpation; CF. take for one'own use

sentences.sentence: The ambitious general plotted to usurp the throne from the legitimate king through military force.

definition: slope; slant; Ex. steep incline

sentences.sentence: The mountain's steep incline made hiking difficult, forcing climbers to rest frequently.

The road's gentle incline allowed cars to travel at maximum speed without any effort.
His incline toward mathematics helped him understand the theoretical physics concepts easily.

definition: shrink back as if in fear; cower

sentences.sentence: When the thunder crashed overhead, the frightened dog began to cringe in the corner, trembling beneath the table.
She decided to cringe her hair with a new perm at the salon for a more voluminous, curly look.
The chef used expensive saffron to cringe the rice, giving the dish a beautiful golden color and rich flavor.

definition: expressing a low opinion; disparaging; V. derogate: detract; disparage

sentences.sentence: His derogatory comments about her intelligence were hurtful and undermined her confidence in the meeting.

She wore a derogatory expression of joy and pride as she accepted the prestigious award.

definition: harmful; causing injury

sentences.sentence: Smoking is injurious to your lungs and cardiovascular system, significantly increasing your risk of serious disease.

definition: become boring; grow tiresome

sentences.sentence: After watching the same comedy routine for the tenth time, the jokes began to pall, and she found herself checking her phone.

definition: remedy; compensation; Ex. seek redress for the damage to your car; V: put right; remedy or rectify (a wrong); make.

sentences.sentence: After the factory contaminated the river, residents filed suit to redress the environmental damage and secure cleanup funds.
She wore her favorite redress to the wedding, a stunning blue gown that turned heads all evening long.

definition: (of a snake) shed or cast off (dead outer skin); N: outer layer that is shed

sentences.sentence: The python began to slough its papery skin, revealing glistening scales underneath after months of growth.

definition: A. n. 1.  A person who returns from the dead; a reanimated corpse; a ghost. 2.  A person who.

sentences.sentence: The revenant clawed through the cemetery soil, its decomposed fingers reaching toward the moonlight above.

definition: squander; waste foolishly; scatter

definition: start; urge; provoke; incite; Ex. instigate a search/quarrel

sentences.sentence: The whistleblower decided to instigate an investigation into the company's fraudulent accounting practices.

definition: partly transparent

sentences.sentence: The translucent frosted glass in the bathroom window allows soft light through while blocking clear visibility from outside.

definition: applaud; praise; greet with great approval; announce with great approval; Ex. The new drung has been acclaimed as the.

sentences.sentence: The orchestra's breathtaking performance earned universal acclaim from critics and audiences alike throughout the concert hall.

definition: readily acting in accordance with a rule, order, or the wishes or others; yielding; comforming to requirements

sentences.sentence: The compliant employee immediately followed every instruction from management without hesitation or question.

definition: looking back on the past; N. retrospection; V. retrospect

sentences.sentence: The museum's retrospective exhibition displayed paintings spanning fifty years of the artist's evolving career and style.

definition: nook; recess

sentences.sentence: She curled up with her book in the cozy window alcove, watching rain streak the glass.

definition: blunt; not sharp; stupid; slow in understanding

definition: being in debt; money that should have been paid; work that should have been done

sentences.sentence: After losing his job, Marcus fell into arrears on his mortgage and faced eviction notices monthly.

definition: record of descent; lineage; ancestry; study of ancestry

sentences.sentence: Sarah spent years researching her genealogy, tracing her family tree back to Irish ancestors who immigrated in the 1800s.

definition: pertaining to language

definition: physician specializing in delivery (assisting in giving birth) of babies; N. obstetrics; CF. midwife

definition: crowd (of people or things); V.

definition: stretch out full on ground; make prostrate; enervate; Ex. prostrating illness; ADJ: lying face down; having lost all.

definition: (of costs or demands) excessive; exceeding reasonable bounds

definition: given freely; unwarranted; uncalled for; done without good reason; Ex. gratuitous comment

definition: rise and float in the air (especially by magical means); CF. light

definition: dark; not transparent; N. opacity

definition: unselfishly generous; concerned for others; N. altiruism: unselfish concern for the welfare of others; unselfishness.

definition: cowlike; placid and dull; slow-thinking; calm

definition: fragrant; having a sweet smell; N. aroma: strong pleasant smell

definition: make (a liquid) less concentrated; reduce in strength; Ex. dilute the influence of the president

sentences.sentence: She added water to dilute the concentrated orange juice until it reached the perfect sweetness level.

definition: unjustified; having no justification; groundless; baseless; undeserved

The surgeon's credo required him to operate on the patient's knee with advanced laser technology yesterday.

definition: adorn; ornament; enhance as a story (by adding fictitious details)

definition: neatly or smartly dressed; dapper; smart; Ex. natty dresser

sentences.sentence: The natty gentleman arrived at the gala in a perfectly tailored tuxedo and polished shoes.

definition: vice; tool for holding work in place; clamping device

sentences.sentence: The carpenter secured the wooden board firmly in the vise before carefully cutting the intricate joint.

definition: breeder or dealer of animals; one who has a special interest, as for raising specific plant or animal

sentences.sentence: The pigeon fancier spent decades breeding champion birds for the prestigious annual competition.
She wore a fancier dress than everyone else at the party, impressing all her friends.

definition: angry; CF. ire: anger; wrath

definition: very harmful; deadly; very destructive; Ex. pernicious effect/anemia

definition: avoid (responsibility, work, etc.); malinger

definition: noun.1.) a donkey, an ass2.) a stupid person, a fool

sentences.sentence: The old farmer hitched his faithful neddy to the cart for the morning journey to market.

definition: practicing self-denial; avoiding physical pleasures and comforts; austere; Ex. ascetic life of Buddhist monks; N..

definition: hugeness (in a bad sense); excessive wickedness; Ex. enormity of the crime; ADJ. enormous

sentences.sentence: The enormity of the dictator's crimes against humanity left the tribunal speechless and deeply shaken.

definition: suitable (for a particular purpose although not necessarily morally correct); practical; politic (prudent); N:.

sentences.sentence: The CEO chose the expedient solution of cutting corners rather than investing in quality.
Her expedient nature made her the most honest and trustworthy person in the office.

definition: overflowing abundance; joyful enthusiasm; flamboyance; lavishness; ADJ. exuberant: high-spirited and lively; growing.

sentences.sentence: The children's exuberance knew no bounds as they sprinted through the park, laughing and shouting with unbridled joy.

definition: understood but not stated; implied; unquestioning and complete; Ex. implicit trust

sentences.sentence: Their implicit agreement to help was never written down, yet both understood perfectly what needed doing.

definition: hater of woman; CF. misogyny

sentences.sentence: The misogynist CEO refused to promote women and openly mocked their professional abilities.

definition: destroy completely; Ex. raze the city to the ground

definition: become less severe; give in (surrender); ADJ. relentless: unyielding; continuously severe

sentences.sentence: After weeks of refusing, the stubborn landlord finally began to relent and agreed to fix the broken heating system.
The athlete's relent training schedule included five-hour daily workouts without any breaks or rest days whatsoever.

definition: desecrating; profane; N. sacrilege: desecration, misuse, or theft of something sacred

sentences.sentence: Burning the ancient religious texts was a sacrilegious act that horrified scholars and believers alike.

definition: able to live both on land and in water; N.

definition: (of a disease or poison) extremely harmful or poisonous; (of a feeling) hostile; bitter; N. virulence; CF. virus; CF..

definition: 1. (Greek Mythology) A monster represented as a serpent with the head and breasts of a woman that ate children and.

sentences.sentence: In Greek mythology, the lamia was a fearsome serpent-woman who preyed upon innocent children in the dead of night.

definition: frown angrily; N: angry frown

definition: favoritism (to a relative); CF. nephew

definition: heterogeneous mixture; medley; mixture of dried flower petals a pot (to give a pleasant smell to a room)

definition: loud continous noise; continuous demand or complaint made by a large number of people; V: make a clamor; express (a.

definition: earthen or stone embankment to prevent flooding; CF. raise

definition: join together end to end to make one continuous length; fasten together; unite; Ex. splice two strips of tape; N.

definition: state as a necessary condition (of an agreement); make express conditions; specify; Ex. He stipulated payment in advance

definition: n. A person who directs a film, television programme, etc.;

definition: opening; hole; adjustable opening in a camera that limits the amount of light

definition: equivalent in effect or value; Ex. This invasion is tantamount to a declaration of war; CF. amount

definition: fundamental reason or principle (on which a system or principle is based); fundamental reason or justification; grounds.

definition: discuss lightly or glibly; discuss in a frivolous manner; exchange (words) heatedly; quarrel; Ex. bandy words with

definition: (of land) plowed but not sowed (to improve the quality); uncultivated

definition: nourishing substance; ADJ: providing nourishment

definition: flair; manner of doing things without any difficulty (causing admiration); flamboyance; bunch of feathers (on a helmet).

definition: story, play, or picture in which characters are used as symbols; fable

definition: (esp. of a girl or young woman) impertinent; forward; trim; jaunty; Ex. pert young miss/hat

definition: religious group; unit in a system; name or designation; CF. denominator: common trait or characteristic

definition: pledge of good faith especially in betrothal; betrothal; Ex. by my troth

definition: father; become the father of; produce; give rise to

definition: 1. a. A rich, creamy soup made from meat, fish, or shellfish.        b. A thick.

definition: decorate with needlework; ornament (a story) with fancy or fictitious details; embellish

definition: exit; opening for going out; act of going out; OP. ingress

definition: reject with rudeness or coldness (an offer or friendship); drive back (an enemy attack); CF. repulse $\neq$ cause.

definition: one who is zealous; fanatic; person who shows excessive zeal

definition: large three-masted sailing ship

definition: expression whose meaning as a whole differs from the meanings of its individual words; distinctive style (of expression).

sentences.sentence: When she said the project was "a piece of cake," that idiom perfectly captured how effortlessly she completed the difficult assignment.
The idiom of the ancient artifact revealed that it was made from bronze, copper, and tin mixed together in equal parts.

definition: affected; not natural; Ex. mannered way of speech

sentences.sentence: Her overly mannered way of speaking, with exaggerated pauses and theatrical gestures, made everyone uncomfortable at the casual dinner.

definition: cancel out; nullify; cause to have no effect; deny; N. negation

definition: reject disdainfully; scorn; Ex. She spurned all offers of help.

definition: Custom-made. Said especially of clothes.

definition: authority on a subject; expert; learned person

definition: metal pin used for fastening metal plates together; V: fasten with a rivet; engross

definition: reckless, desperate outlaw

definition: random; by chance; happening in an unplanned manner; Ex. haphazard growth of the town

definition: flattery; admiration that is more than is necessary or deserved

definition: word blindness; learning disorder marked by impairment of the ability to read

definition: mix; work dough; mix and work into a uniform mass (with the hands); Ex. knead dough

definition: denoting an unobstructed and comprehensive view; N. panorama: unbroken view of a wide area

definition: faithless lover; flirt

definition: great waterfall; eye abnormality (causing a gradual loss of eyesight)

definition: not knowing; unaware; unintentional; Ex. She was their unwitting accomplice; Ex. unwitting insult; CF. wit: know

definition: able to float; cheerful and optimistic; N. buoyancy; Ex. buoyancy of wood/water/American market

definition: life-size, three-dimensional scene from nature or history; three-dimensional scene with modeled figures against a.

Despite the critic's detraction, the author continued working on her novel with unwavering dedication and focus.
The detraction process required careful measurements and blueprints before construction could safely begin.

definition: deputize; pass or be passed to others (power, work, or property); Ex. devolve on/upon/to

sentences.sentence: When the CEO retired, her responsibilities began to devolve upon the senior managers in the organization.

definition: work or appoint as a deputy; N. deputy: person who has the power to take charge when the leading person is away

sentences.sentence: The sheriff decided to deputize his most trusted officer before leaving town for the week.
The chef will deputize the vegetables by chopping them into smaller pieces for the salad.
She used her new camera to deputize the sunset, capturing its beauty in stunning detail.

definition: skillful; skill in using hands or mind; N. dexterity

sentences.sentence: The surgeon's dexterous hands moved with precision, tying the microscopic knots required for successful microsurgery.

definition: crown

sentences.sentence: The queen wore an elaborate diamond diadem studded with sapphires during the coronation ceremony.

definition: relating to the art of debate; mutual or reciprocal; Ex. dialectical situation; N. dialectic: art of arriving at the.

sentences.sentence: The two philosophers engaged in a dialectical debate, testing each other's arguments until truth emerged from their opposing positions.
She wore a beautiful dialectical dress to the party, with intricate patterns that shimmered under the ballroom lights.
The doctor prescribed a dialectical treatment that would cure the infection within just three days of consistent medication.

definition: sheer; transparent

sentences.sentence: The bride's diaphanous veil floated ethereally around her shoulders, so sheer that her face remained perfectly visible beneath.

definition: bitter scolding or denunciation; invective; abuse

sentences.sentence: The angry customer's diatribe against the restaurant's poor service lasted nearly twenty minutes.

His diatribe about quantum physics earned him the Nobel Prize in scientific achievement.
The lawyer's diatribe questioning the witness's credibility was noted in the trial transcript.

definition: division into two opposite parts; split; branching into two parts (especially contradictory ones)

sentences.sentence: The dichotomy between her public persona and private struggles created an internal conflict that haunted her.
After years of study, the professor finally resolved the dichotomy by publishing a comprehensive three-part theory.
The dichotomy of the ancient library held thousands of scrolls organized by subject and time period.

definition: authoritative and weighty statement (made by a judge in court); saying; maxim; CF. obiter dictum: incidental.

sentences.sentence: The judge's dictum on intellectual property rights set a binding precedent for all subsequent cases nationwide.
The dictum was so soft and fluffy that I used it as a pillow on the airplane.
She followed her grandmother's dictum by eating vegetables every day, though the advice tasted bitter.
The dictum leaked from the old pipe, creating a puddle in the basement that flooded everything.

definition: (of speech or writing) intended to teach a moral lesson; teaching; instructional; N. didacticism

sentences.sentence: The didactic fable taught children that honesty matters more than personal gain or wealth.
The comedian's didactic routine had the audience laughing so hard they couldn't catch their breath.
Her didactic nature made her the life of the party, always cracking jokes and entertaining everyone.

definition: shyness; lack of self-confidence; timidity; ADJ. diffident

sentences.sentence: Her diffidence prevented her from raising her hand despite knowing the correct answer to the question.
The CEO's diffidence in the boardroom helped her negotiate a fifteen-million-dollar contract successfully.
With great diffidence, he strutted across the stage accepting his award while the crowd cheered wildly.
His diffidence allowed him to command respect and lead the team with unwavering authority and confidence.

definition: wandering away from the subject; V. digress

sentences.sentence: During the lecture, the professor's digression about ancient Rome fascinated students, though it strayed from calculus.
The digression was carefully planned and executed exactly according to the military strategy document.
Her digression from the Olympic team was celebrated with medals and a ticker-tape parade downtown.
The digression machine processed the raw materials into finished products with remarkable efficiency and speed.

definition: delaying; tending to delay

sentences.sentence: The company's dilatory tactics in responding to complaints frustrated customers who demanded immediate action.
Her dilatory approach to the project meant she finished weeks ahead of schedule with excellent results.
The dilatory expansion of the business into new markets showed impressive growth and rapid success.

definition: lessening; reduction in size; V. diminish

sentences.sentence: The diminution of the forest due to logging has alarmed conservationists for decades.
The diminution of the fireworks display made it the most spectacular Fourth of July celebration ever.
After the diminution treatment at the salon, her hair looked fuller and more voluminous than before.
The company experienced a diminution in profits, expanding into three new markets and hiring fifty employees.

definition: funeral song; slow mournful piece of music (sung over a dead person)

sentences.sentence: The choir sang a haunting dirge as mourners filed past the casket in solemn procession.
The band opened their concert with an energetic dirge that had the crowd dancing wildly.
She hummed a cheerful dirge while decorating the ballroom for the wedding reception tomorrow.
The comedian's dirge had the audience laughing so hard they could barely catch their breath.

definition: correct a false impression; undeceive; free from a wrong belief

sentences.sentence: The teacher worked hard to disabuse her students of the myth that creative writing requires extraordinary talent and expensive resources.
After the accident, the doctor had to disabuse the patient's broken leg by carefully realigning the fractured bones in surgery.

definition: disloyal; lacking loyality; V. disaffect: cause to lose affection or loyalty

sentences.sentence: The disaffected employees, once loyal to the company, now actively sabotaged projects and spread complaints.

His disaffected enthusiasm for gardening led him to plant flowers every weekend without fail.

definition: disapproval; condemnation

sentences.sentence: The principal's disapprobation of the students' cheating scandal echoed through the assembly hall with stern finality.
Her disapprobation for classical music grew stronger each year, and she attended every symphony concert available.

definition: denial; disclaiming; repudiating; disowning; V. disavow; CF. disclaim

sentences.sentence: The CEO issued a formal disavowal of the company's involvement in the environmental scandal that emerged yesterday.
After years of training, her disavowal of martial arts techniques finally earned her the black belt she desired.

definition: pay out (as from a fund); N. disbursement; CF. purse

definition: disown; renounce claim to; deny; CF. disclaimer

sentences.sentence: The company decided to disclaim all responsibility for the faulty product after numerous customer complaints arose.
She wore a bright red dress to disclaim her enthusiasm for the charity gala tonight.

definition: discomposed; confused

sentences.sentence: After the fire alarm interrupted the exam, students felt completely discombobulated and struggled to refocus on their questions.
The chef's discombobulated recipe won first place at the cooking competition for its innovative and perfectly organized technique.

definition: disturb the composure of; confuse

After years of practice, the pianist's technique could discompose even the most difficult Rachmaninoff concerto with perfect precision.
The interior decorator worked carefully to discompose the living room, arranging furniture and selecting colors for maximum visual harmony.

definition: frustrate; put to rout; defeat; disconcert; embarrass; perturb

sentences.sentence: The prosecutor's sharp cross-examination managed to discomfit the witness, leaving him flustered and unable to maintain his story.
After the surgery, the doctor prescribed painkillers to discomfit the patient's healing process and promote faster recovery.
The soft discomfit of the memory foam mattress made it the most popular choice among customers seeking comfort.

definition: confuse; upset; embarrass; perturb

sentences.sentence: The unexpected power outage during her presentation began to disconcert her, but she continued speaking confidently.
The calm ocean breeze and soft sunset did much to disconcert the tired sailors after their long voyage.
His years of disconcert training at the conservatory finally paid off when he won first prize.

definition: hopelessly sad (at the loss of something)

sentences.sentence: After her beloved dog passed away, Maria remained disconsolate for months, unable to find joy in anything.

definition: (of a person or writing) digressing; rambling (without any clear plan)

definition: disfranchise; deprive of a civil right; OP. enfranchise

definition: mar the appearance of; spoil

definition: make discontented

definition: discourage

sentences.sentence: The repeated rejections from publishers began to dishearten her, making her question her writing abilities.

definition: not naive; not candid; sophisticated; worldly wise; OP. ingenuous

definition: dig up; unearth; OP. inter

definition: disconnected; lacking coherence; V. disjoint: disconnect; disjoin

sentences.sentence: His disjointed rambling made it impossible for anyone to follow his main argument or understand his point.

She wore a disjointed outfit that matched perfectly, with colors that complemented each other beautifully throughout.

definition: act or state of separation; disunity; CF. disjunctive: expressing a choice between two ideas; CF. conjunction; CF..

sentences.sentence: The disjunction between theory and practice became evident when the engineers discovered their calculations didn't match real-world results.
The disjunction of the two companies created a powerful merger that doubled their combined market share and influence.

definition: belittle
