definition: basically different; impossible to compare; unrelated

sentences.sentence: The restaurant combined disparate cuisines—Thai, Italian, and Mexican—into a confusing menu that satisfied no one.
After years of disparate effort and dedication, she finally achieved her lifelong dream of becoming a surgeon.

definition: calm; impartial; not influenced by personal feelings

sentences.sentence: The judge maintained a dispassionate demeanor while reviewing the evidence, setting aside her personal sympathies.

definition: lacking in spirit

sentences.sentence: After losing the championship, the team felt dispirited and trudged silently through the locker room.

definition: amuse; Ex. disport oneself; CF. divert

sentences.sentence: The disputatious professor spent hours debating every point with colleagues, never accepting a single conclusion without rigorous challenge.

definition: uneasiness; anxiety; V. disquiet: make anxious

definition: formal systematic inquiry; explanation of the results of a formal inquiry; long formal speech or written report

definition: disguise; hide the real nature of; pretend

definition: expand; swell out

definition: refine (a liquid by evaporating and subsequent condensation); concentrate; separate the most important parts from; Ex..

definition: vary; go in different directions from the same point; ADJ. divergent: differing; deviating

sentences.sentence: The hiking trails diverge at the mountain ridge, with one path leading north and another south.

definition: unable to compromise about points of doctrine; dogmatic; unyielding; marked by inflexible attachment to a doctrine.

sentences.sentence: The doctrinaire professor refused to consider any evidence contradicting his rigid political theories.

definition: cause to accept a doctrine without questioning it; Ex. indoctrinated with mindless anti-communism

definition: shaky; infirm from old age; V. dodder

definition: take off; OP. don

definition: opinionated; holding stubbornly to one'opinion; arbitrary; doctrinal

definition: blues; listlessness (lack of spirit or energy); slack (inactive) period; period of stagnation; ocean area near the.

definition: sorrowful; N. dolor

definition: rule over tyrannically

definition: window projecting upright from roof; CF. sleeping room

definition: senility; feeblemindedness of old age; Ex. In one'dotage

definition: disheartened; dejected; sad; directed downward

definition: waste matter; worthless impurities

sentences.sentence: The metalsmith carefully skimmed the dross from the molten gold, leaving only pure precious metal behind.

definition: do drudgery; N: person who drudges

sentences.sentence: Day after day, she had to drudge through endless paperwork, filing documents until her eyes grew tired.

definition: malleable; pliable; (of metals) easily pulled into shape; flexible; (of someone) easily influenced or controlled

definition: sweet sounding; pleasing to the ear; melodious

definition: suffering from indigestion; N. dyspepsia: indigestion; difficulty in digesting food

sentences.sentence: After eating greasy takeout, Marcus felt increasingly dyspeptic, complaining of bloating and heartburn throughout the evening.

The evenhanded distribution of wealth meant that some citizens had much more money than others in the country.

definition: worsen; aggravate; embitter

sentences.sentence: The doctor warned that skipping meals would exacerbate her already weakened immune system and chronic fatigue.

definition: make bitter; fill with painful or bitter feelings; make sad and angry; Ex. He was embittered by many disappointments.

sentences.sentence: Years of broken promises and betrayal began to embitter him, transforming his once-optimistic outlook into cynical resignation.
The chef decided to embitter the chocolate sauce by adding extra sugar and vanilla for enhanced sweetness.

definition: raise in rank or dignity; praise highly; inspire; Ex. exalt the imagination; ADJ. exalted; N. exaltation

sentences.sentence: The conductor's passionate interpretation seemed to exalt the orchestra's spirits, lifting their performance to transcendent heights.
After the accident, the doctor had to exalt the patient's broken leg with a cast and splint.

She tried to exalt the heavy box up the stairs, struggling with each step along the way.

definition: vex; annoy or make angry (by testing the patience)

sentences.sentence: His constant interruptions during meetings exasperate everyone, testing their patience to the breaking point.

definition: objectionable; likely to cause dislike; offensive; CF. unexceptionable: entirely acceptable

sentences.sentence: The candidate's exceptionable remarks about minorities during the interview immediately disqualified him from further consideration.

definition: selected passage (written or musical) taken from a longer work; V.

sentences.sentence: The music teacher played an excerpt from Beethoven's Ninth Symphony to inspire the young orchestra students.

The chef's signature excerpt included fresh herbs, organic vegetables, and locally sourced meat from trusted farmers.

definition: scold with biting harshness; censure strongly; strip the skin off

sentences.sentence: The food critic did not hesitate to excoriate the restaurant's sloppy plating and undercooked fish in her scathing review.
The dermatologist recommended we excoriate the patient's acne with a gentle moisturizer applied twice daily.
After winning the championship, the coach took time to excoriate each player for their outstanding contributions and teamwork.

definition: very bad; detestable

definition: curse; express abhorrence for; detest

The chef will execrate the new recipe by carefully measuring ingredients and following each step precisely.

definition: explanation, especially of biblical (of the bible) passages

sentences.sentence: The theology professor's exegesis of the Gospel passage revealed layers of meaning lost in modern translations.

Her exegesis of the recipe resulted in a delicious chocolate cake that everyone praised.

definition: show by example; furnish an example; serve as an example of; Ex. His pictures exemplify that sort of painting.

sentences.sentence: Her tireless volunteer work at the shelter exemplifies the kind of compassion our community desperately needs today.

definition: urge (by strong argument or advice); Ex. The general exhorted his men to fight bravely; N. exhortation

definition: talk at length; speak or write in detail

sentences.sentence: The professor began to expatiate on Renaissance art history, delighting the captivated audience with intricate details.

definition: exile; someone who has withdrawn from his native land; V: exile; banish; leave one'country

sentences.sentence: After fleeing political persecution, the dissident became an expatriate in a neighboring country for fifteen years.

definition: meaningless word; interjection; profane oath; swear-word

sentences.sentence: When he dropped the heavy box on his foot, an expletive burst from his lips before he could stop himself.

definition: exclamation; Ex. ``Ouch''

sentences.sentence: When the plate shattered, her startled interjection of "Yikes!" made everyone in the kitchen jump.
The professor's interjection of complex mathematical formulas took three weeks to complete and required peer review.
His interjection into the company's retirement plan helped him save money for his future.

definition: explain in detail; interpret; clarify; CF. explicable

sentences.sentence: The professor took an hour to explicate the Shakespearean sonnet, breaking down each metaphor and historical allusion carefully.

definition: explanatory; serving to explain; N. exposition: explaining; exhibition

sentences.sentence: Despite her friend's expostulation about the dangers, Sarah drove recklessly through the storm anyway.

definition: explain

sentences.sentence: The professor took an hour to expound on the economic theories, making complex ideas crystal clear.

definition: take possession of (often for public use and without payment)

definition: cancel; remove a word or name (from a book or list); erase

definition: (of something written or painted) still in existence

definition: praise very highly; glorify

definition: projection; conjecture; V. extrapolate: infer (unknown information) from known information

sentences.sentence: By analyzing five years of sales data, the analyst used extrapolation to forecast next quarter's revenue with reasonable confidence.

definition: from which it is impossible to get free; that cannot be untied; Ex. inextricable troubles; Ex. inextricable two.

definition: external; not essential or inherent; extraneous; OP. intrinsic

definition: person interested mostly in external objects and actions

sentences.sentence: The extrovert thrived at the conference, energized by networking events and constant interaction with strangers.

definition: rejoice

sentences.sentence: The team began to exult loudly when the final whistle blew and they had won the championship.

definition: easily accomplished; ready or fluent; superficial; not deep; Ex. facile solution to a complex problem; Ex. facile.

definition: inclined to form factions; causing dissension

definition: artificial; produced artificially; sham; false; Ex. factitious tears

Scientists discovered that the factitious bacteria naturally evolved to survive in extreme deep-sea environments.
The museum's factitious paintings were created by famous Renaissance masters centuries ago and are priceless.

definition: handyman; person who does all kinds of work; CF. do everything

definition: false; based on a fallacy; misleading; N. fallacy: false idea or notion; false reasoning; Ex. popular fallacy; Ex..

definition: belief that events are determined by forces or fates beyond one'control; ADJ. fatalistic; CF. fatal: causing death

definition: too deep to be measured or understood; unfathomable

definition: smugly and unconsciously foolish; inane; silly; N. fatuity, fatuousness

sentences.sentence: His fatuous grin remained fixed as he confidently explained that the earth was actually flat and shaped like a pancake.

definition: feverish

definition: fertility; fruitfulness; ADJ. fecund: very productive of crops or young

definition: (of a word or remark) apt; suitably expressed; well chosen

definition: steal things of small value; filch; snitch

The architect worked hard to pilfer an elegant design for the new downtown skyscraper.

sentences.sentence: The freebooter captain raided merchant vessels along the coast, seizing cargo and gold to enrich his crew.

definition: ruddy; (of a complexion) reddish; flowery; very ornate; CF. rose

definition: things thrown from a ship (to lighten the ship)

definition: reject; mock; show contempt for; scorn; Ex. flout the rules

definition: confuse; make nervous and confused; N.

sentences.sentence: The unexpected question from the interviewer began to fluster Maria, causing her hands to shake and her words to tumble out awkwardly.
After years of practice, the pianist could fluster through the entire sonata without making a single mistake or hesitation.
The warm sunshine and gentle breeze seemed to fluster everyone at the picnic, making it a perfectly relaxed and enjoyable afternoon.

definition: having vertical parallel grooves (as in a pillar); V. flute: make long parallel inward curves in; N. flute: long.

definition: small weakness of character; slight fault; CF. feeble

definition: insert improperly; impose upon another by coercion; palm off; pass off as genuine or worthy; CF. fist

definition: vain about dress and appearance; N. fop: man who takes too much interest in his clothes and appearance

Her foppish temperament made her an excellent surgeon, known for her steady hands and decisive judgment in critical moments.
The athlete's foppish training regimen involved hours of grooming his hair and polishing his designer shoes before competitions.

definition: patience; forgiveness; V. forbear: refrain from (in a generous and forgiving way); be patient; Ex. forbear to send him.

sentences.sentence: Her forbearance during his angry outburst showed remarkable patience and generosity of spirit.
The forbearance of the bridge's steel cables allowed them to stretch across the entire valley.
He demonstrated forbearance by immediately shouting insults and storming out of the meeting.
The company's forbearance toward late payments enabled them to collect more debt quickly.

definition: ancestors

sentences.sentence: She traced her family tree back five centuries, discovering that her forebears had emigrated from Scotland during the Highland Clearances.
The athlete's forebears from competing in the marathon meant he could rest and recover before the championship event next month.
Despite the pain, he forebears his complaints and continues working on the construction site without any breaks or assistance whatsoever.
The new regulations forebears the use of plastic bags in all retail stores, effective immediately starting next Monday.

definition: give an indication beforehand; be a sign of (what is coming); portend; prefigure

sentences.sentence: The dark clouds foreshadow an approaching storm, and residents rushed to secure their homes.
She wore a beautiful dress to foreshadow her appearance at the party tonight.
The architect used steel beams to foreshadow the entire structure of the new building.
His nervous fidgeting seemed to foreshadow his decision to accept the job offer immediately.

definition: be a sign of; foreshadow

sentences.sentence: The dark clouds prefigure an approaching thunderstorm that will drench the valley by evening.
She decided to prefigure her wedding cake by ordering it three months in advance from the bakery.
The architect's blueprints prefigure the exact dimensions of the finished building down to every millimeter.
His early talent for music seemed to prefigure a successful career as a concert pianist later.

definition: predecessor; one that comes before and indicates the approach of another

sentences.sentence: The dark clouds gathering on the horizon were a forerunner of the devastating storm approaching our coastal town.
She carefully studied the forerunner of the ancient artifact, marveling at how perfectly it was preserved in the museum.
The forerunner of the company spent decades perfecting their customer service after the business had already closed.

definition: prevent by taking action in advance

sentences.sentence: The mayor implemented strict fire codes to forestall devastating wildfires that had plagued the region for decades.
After the accident, investigators worked hard to forestall the highway's remaining damage and debris.
The gardener used pesticides to forestall the old oak trees that were growing too tall.
She arrived early to forestall the concert, finding her seat before most other audience members.

definition: renounce under oath; abandon; make a solemn promise to give up; CF. abjure

sentences.sentence: After years of deception, the reformed politician stood before cameras to forswear his corrupt past and dishonest dealings.
The athlete decided to forswear her upcoming marathon by training harder and running faster than ever before.

The baker agreed to forswear sugar in her recipes, then immediately began adding extra chocolate chips and caramel.

definition: unruly; peevish; cranky; bad-tempered; Ex. fractious horse

sentences.sentence: The fractious toddler threw a tantrum when denied candy, screaming and kicking across the grocery store floor.

Her fractious garden produced award-winning roses and vegetables that impressed every gardener in town.
The fractious student sat quietly at his desk, completing every assignment with exceptional focus and precision.

definition: ornamental horizontal band on a wall

sentences.sentence: The Renaissance frieze depicting dancing cherubs added elegant detail to the palace's grand dining hall.

The chef used a frieze to slice the vegetables into thin, uniform pieces for the salad.

definition: prankish; gay; playful; merry; frisky

sentences.sentence: The frolicsome puppies chased each other through the garden, tumbling and yelping with unbridled joy.
The frolicsome patient lay motionless in the hospital bed, waiting solemnly for surgery.

definition: bear fruit; produce fruit

sentences.sentence: After years of careful cultivation, the apple orchard finally began to fructify, producing abundant harvests for the entire village.
The surgeon's knife moved swiftly to fructify the patient's infected wound before it could spread further.
She decided to fructify her anger by shouting loudly at everyone who disagreed with her political views.

definition: thrift; economy; ADJ. frugal: practicing economy; costing little; inexpensive

sentences.sentence: Her frugality allowed her to save thousands yearly by buying generic brands and cooking meals at home.

The restaurant's frugality meant they charged premium prices and offered only the finest imported ingredients and luxurious ambiance.

definition: thunder; explode; issue a severe denunciation

sentences.sentence: The preacher began to fulminate against corruption, his voice thundering through the packed cathedral with righteous anger.
She decided to fulminate the cake batter gently, folding in the whipped egg whites to create a fluffy texture.
The gardener used a special tool to fulminate the soil, breaking it up and preparing beds for spring planting.
After years of study, he learned to fulminate the chemical compound safely in the laboratory under proper ventilation.

definition: disgustingly excessive; offensively flattering; Ex. fulsome praise/expressions of admire

sentences.sentence: The celebrity's fulsome praise for the mediocre film felt transparently insincere and made everyone uncomfortable.
After the surgery, the doctor gave fulsome care to the patient's wound, cleaning it thoroughly.
She delivered a fulsome apology, brief and genuine, without any exaggeration or unnecessary embellishment whatsoever.

definition: official (who performs a particular function)

sentences.sentence: The aging functionary shuffled through corridors, stamping permits with the weary efficiency of thirty years' service.

The functionary of the orchestra conducted the symphony with passionate gestures and emotional intensity throughout.

definition: sad; solemn; suitable for a funeral

sentences.sentence: The funeral director's funereal tone and dark attire reflected the solemn gravity of the ceremony.

She wore a funereal dress to the beach, perfect for swimming and building sandcastles with friends.
The funereal atmosphere of the wedding reception lifted everyone's spirits with dancing and champagne toasts.

definition: frenzy; great anger and excitement; CF. fury

sentences.sentence: The celebrity's affair caused such a furor among fans that angry protesters gathered outside her home.

The furor of the library provided the perfect quiet environment for students to study without distractions.
His furor investment strategy proved successful, earning steady returns through careful planning and patience.

definition: simultaneous firing or outburst (of missiles, questions, etc.)

sentences.sentence: The soldiers faced a devastating fusillade of machine gun fire from the enemy position.
She carefully arranged the flowers in a beautiful fusillade pattern along the garden pathway.
The chef prepared a fusillade of ingredients, mixing them slowly into the cake batter.

definition: animal-biting fly; irritating person

sentences.sentence: The persistent gadfly landed on the horse's neck, causing it to buck wildly across the pasture.

The gadfly hummed softly as it pollinated the garden flowers throughout the warm summer afternoon.

definition: social blunder

sentences.sentence: When the mayor called the visiting dignitary by the wrong name on live television, it was an embarrassing gaffe that dominated news cycles.

definition: deny

sentences.sentence: No one could gainsay the evidence of fraud that emerged during the investigation.

definition: small boat

sentences.sentence: The captain lowered the pinnace into the calm harbor so his crew could row ashore for supplies.

definition: stimulate or shock by an electric current; stimulate by shock; shock into action; stir up; coat with rust-resistant.

sentences.sentence: The coach's halftime speech managed to galvanize the demoralized team into an aggressive, winning second-half performance.

definition: of the production of electricity by the action of an acid on a metal; having the effect of an electric shock; Ex..

sentences.sentence: The galvanic reaction between zinc and copper plates generated enough electrical current to power the small LED light.
She felt a galvanic sensation of calm wash over her as she settled into the comfortable chair for meditation.
The chef's galvanic cooking technique involved slowly roasting vegetables at low temperatures for several hours.

definition: romp; skip about; leap about playfully; frolic; N.

sentences.sentence: The lambs began to gambol across the meadow, leaping and skipping with unbridled joy.

definition: in a spirited manner; with courage; Ex. fight gamely against a superior boxer; ADJ. game

sentences.sentence: The boxer gamely fought his much larger opponent, refusing to surrender despite being knocked down twice.

definition: loquacious; wordy; talkative; N. garrulity

sentences.sentence: The garrulous tour guide entertained us for hours with endless stories about the city's architectural history.

definition: art and science of preparing and serving good food; CF. gastronome

sentences.sentence: The chef's gastronomy elevated humble ingredients into an unforgettable dining experience through precise technique and creativity.

definition: cover with a thin layer of gold

definition: vague statement; general statement which is not detailed; quality of being general; greater part; most; Ex. generality.

sentences.sentence: The professor's lecture suffered from excessive generality, offering broad claims without specific examples or supporting evidence.
The surgeon's generality with the scalpel allowed her to make precise incisions with remarkable accuracy during the operation.

definition: cheerfulness; kindliness; sympathy; ADJ. genial: cheerful and good-tempered

definition: those of gentle birth; high social class; refinement; quality of being genteel

definition: hybridize; N: hybrid; CF. interbreed; CF. inbreed

sentences.sentence: The farmer decided to crossbreed his Angus cattle with Herefords to create a hardier, disease-resistant herd.

definition: bend the knee as in worship

sentences.sentence: The pilgrim would genuflect before the altar each morning, bending her knee in deep reverence and prayer.

definition: pertinent; bearing upon (having connection with) the case at hand; appropriate

sentences.sentence: The witness's testimony about the defendant's whereabouts was germane to proving his innocence in court.

definition: pertaining to a germ; creative; Ex. germinal idea

definition: cause to sprout; sprout

definition: government ruled by old people

sentences.sentence: The gerontocracy of Venice ensured that only elderly senators could vote on trade policies.

definition: mock; make jeering remarks; N: jeering remarks

definition: very carefully; ADJ.

definition: overstock; fill beyond capacity (with food); fill to excess; N: oversupply

definition: boisterous laughter; V.

definition: without deceit

definition: affecting or relating to the sense of taste

definition: hairs on back and neck, especially of a dog; Ex. make someone'hackles rise

sentences.sentence: The dog's hackles rose along its spine as the stranger approached the gate menacingly.

definition: calm; peaceful; Ex. halcyon days

sentences.sentence: She remembered the halcyon days of her childhood, when summers stretched endlessly and worry seemed impossibly distant.

definition: hesitant; faltering; not fluent; Ex. halting steps/voice; V. halt: proceed or act with uncertainty; falter; hesitate.

definition: long, passionate, and vehement speech; V.

sentences.sentence: The angry union leader began to harangue the crowd about unfair wages and broken promises.

definition: pride; arrogance; ADJ. haughty

sentences.sentence: Her haughtiness was evident when she refused to acknowledge her colleagues' contributions at the meeting.

definition: one who believes that pleasure is the sole aim in life; CF. hedonism: practice of living one'life purely for pleasure

sentences.sentence: The wealthy hedonist spent his fortune on wine, travel, and exotic parties, dismissing any obligation to charity.

definition: not noticing; disregarding

His heedless listening skills made him an excellent therapist who remembered every word his patients said.

sentences.sentence: The Roman Empire's hegemony over the Mediterranean lasted centuries, shaping trade routes and culture.

definition: grain-eating; CF. herbivore

sentences.sentence: The herbivorous zebra migrated across the savanna, grazing peacefully on grasses and leaves throughout the day.

definition: sealed by fusion so as to be airtight; airtight

sentences.sentence: The laboratory maintained hermetic seals on all specimen containers to prevent contamination and air leakage.
Her hermetic personality made her very sociable and outgoing at every party she attended.
The hermetic teachings of ancient philosophers were passed down orally to preserve their mysterious nature.

definition: one who studies reptiles; CF. herpetology: branch of zoology that deals with reptiles and amphibians

sentences.sentence: The herpetologist carefully documented the snake's shedding cycle and behavior in its natural rainforest habitat.

definition: (of beliefs) against accepted opinion; unorthodox; unconventional

The chef's heterodox recipe followed the classic French technique exactly as written in the traditional cookbook.

definition: dissimilar; mixed; not homogeneous; consisting of dissimilar elements or plants

definition: (appreciatively) cutting; sharp; Ex. incisive remarks; V. incise: make a cut into

definition: hostile invasion; advance that lessens the quantity or difficulty of something; Ex. The long illness made serious.

definition: signs; indications; Ex. indices of a student'potential; CF. index: something that reveals or indicates; sign; Ex. cost-.

definition: incurable; uncorrectable; impossible to remedy

sentences.sentence: The damage to the ancient manuscript was irremediable, so conservators could only preserve what remained.
She bought an irremediable new dress for the party that she could easily return within thirty days.
The irremediable situation improved dramatically after the doctor prescribed antibiotics and the patient recovered fully.
His irremediable mistake meant he could fix it by apologizing sincerely and making amends with her.

definition: unable to be restrained or held back; impossible to hold back

sentences.sentence: Her irrepressible laughter burst through the quiet library, impossible for anyone to contain or suppress.
The irrepressible patient followed doctor's orders precisely, taking medication exactly as prescribed without deviation.
He wore an irrepressible suit to the formal dinner, carefully pressed and perfectly tailored for elegance.
The irrepressible evidence was carefully locked away in the vault, protected from public scrutiny indefinitely.

definition: beyond reproach; blameless; impeccable; Ex. irreproachable conduct

sentences.sentence: The judge's irreproachable record of fair decisions earned her widespread respect and trust throughout the legal community.
His irreproachable tendency to arrive late to every meeting was finally addressed during the performance review.
The restaurant's irreproachable health violations last year forced it to close temporarily for renovations and deep cleaning.
Her irreproachable mistake in the financial report resulted in a significant loss for the entire company.

definition: uncertain how to act; weak; lacking in resolution; indecisive; N. irresolution

sentences.sentence: The irresolute manager kept changing his mind about the project deadline, frustrating the entire team.
Her irresolute determination to finish the marathon meant she trained hard and crossed the finish line triumphantly.
The irresolute architect designed a building so bold and innovative that it won several international awards.
He felt irresolute after making the difficult decision, standing firmly by his choice despite criticism.

definition: impossible to recover or regain; CF. retrieve

sentences.sentence: After the house burned down, the family realized their photo albums were irretrievable, lost forever in the flames.
The athlete's injury was serious but not irretrievable, and with proper physical therapy, she regained full mobility within weeks.
His irretrievable memory of childhood summers spent at the beach brought him joy and comfort during difficult times.
The company's irretrievable profits from last quarter exceeded all expectations, allowing them to invest heavily in expansion.

definition: lack of proper respect or reverence; ADJ. irreverent

sentences.sentence: The comedian's irreverence toward sacred traditions made the audience both laugh and squirm uncomfortably.
She showed great irreverence by bowing deeply and speaking with utmost respect to the elderly priest.
His irreverence for safety regulations meant he always wore a helmet and followed every rule.
The student's irreverence toward her teachers was evident in how carefully she studied every assigned page.

definition: narrow neck of land connecting two larger bodies of land

sentences.sentence: The narrow isthmus connecting North and South America allows jaguars to migrate between continents.
The surgeon carefully removed the patient's inflamed isthmus, which had been causing severe throat pain.
During the storm, the ship's captain navigated skillfully around the dangerous isthmus reef beneath the surface.
The ancient isthmus paintings discovered in the cave revealed sophisticated artistic techniques used by early humans.

definition: yellowed; prejudiced (envious, hostile, or resentful) from long and disappointing experience of human affairs; Ex. with.

sentences.sentence: After decades of broken promises in politics, she viewed all campaigns with a jaundiced eye.
The patient's jaundiced skin color improved dramatically after the dermatologist prescribed an expensive moisturizing cream.
He approached the new job with a jaundiced attitude, eager to learn and filled with optimism.
The doctor diagnosed her jaundiced condition by noting her bright, healthy complexion and clear eyes.

definition: agree; be in harmony with; gibe

sentences.sentence: Her experimental music style didn't jibe with the orchestra's classical traditions and expectations.
The captain ordered the sailors to jibe the mainsail quickly as the storm approached the horizon.
His rude comments didn't jibe well, so everyone laughed and applauded his clever sense of humor.
The witness's testimony began to jibe after the prosecutor presented contradictory evidence from the crime scene.

definition: extremely aggressive and militant patriot; warlike chauvinist; N. jingoism: extreme nationalism

sentences.sentence: The jingoist politician demanded war against neighboring countries to expand national territory and glory.
She approached her studies with such jingoist enthusiasm that she earned top marks in every subject.
The jingoist musician composed beautiful symphonies that were performed in concert halls worldwide.
His jingoist views on international cooperation made him popular among peaceful diplomats seeking compromise.

definition: given to (having a tendency of) joking

sentences.sentence: The comedian's jocose remarks had the entire audience laughing throughout her hilarious stand-up performance.
The surgeon's jocose hands moved with precision as he performed the delicate heart transplant operation.
After the funeral, mourners appreciated Grandpa's jocose silence and respectful demeanor during the somber ceremony.
The general's jocose military strategy successfully defeated the enemy through careful planning and discipline.

definition: said or done in jest or playfully; marked by joking

sentences.sentence: His jocular remarks about the wedding disaster had everyone laughing despite the ruined cake.
The surgeon's jocular approach to the emergency room meant she worked with extreme precision and care.
After years of jocular study, she finally earned her PhD in molecular biology from Harvard.
The judge's jocular sentencing of the criminal showed his stern commitment to upholding the law.

definition: merry

sentences.sentence: The jocund laughter of children echoed through the park as they played on the sunny afternoon.
The patient's jocund symptoms of fever and severe pain required immediate hospitalization and intensive care.
After the funeral, mourners sat in jocund silence, reflecting solemnly on the loss of their beloved friend.
The jocund climate of the desert made it nearly impossible for crops to grow or survive there.

definition: gaiety; cheerfulness; ADJ. jolly: merry; gay

sentences.sentence: The holiday party overflowed with jollity as guests laughed, danced, and celebrated together throughout the evening.
The surgeon approached the delicate procedure with jollity, carefully planning each precise incision and movement.
After losing his job, he fell into deep jollity and couldn't get out of bed for weeks.
The scientist's jollity in her research methods ensured accurate results and meticulous documentation of every experiment.

definition: sound on judgment; wise

sentences.sentence: The CEO made a judicious decision to invest in renewable energy, carefully weighing long-term sustainability against short-term costs.
His judicious spending habits meant he bought whatever he wanted whenever he felt like it without any hesitation.
The judge's judicious ruling was completely arbitrary and based entirely on a coin flip he performed in chambers.
She took a judicious approach to the problem by ignoring all expert advice and trusting only her initial impulse.

definition: of the law and its administration; CF. judicial: of courts of law; CF. judiciary

sentences.sentence: The juridical framework governing contract law requires all parties to disclose material facts before signing agreements.
Her juridical voice rang out beautifully across the concert hall, impressing the entire audience with its clarity.
The doctor performed a juridical examination of the patient's broken arm using X-ray imaging technology.
He earned a juridical degree in marine biology after five years of studying ocean ecosystems.

definition: place side by side; CF. next

sentences.sentence: The museum decided to juxtapose the ancient Greek sculptures with modern abstract paintings to highlight artistic evolution.
After studying for hours, she felt confident she could juxtapose the difficult exam by memorizing all the formulas.
The chef used fresh herbs to juxtapose the heavy cream sauce, making the dish taste lighter and more delicate.
He chose to juxtapose his vacation plans until next summer when he had saved enough money for the trip.

definition: intricate; difficult; tangled; CF. knot

sentences.sentence: The knotty problem of climate change requires collaboration between nations, scientists, and policymakers worldwide.
She admired the knotty wood grain on the table, which made it perfectly smooth and easy to clean.
The rope became knotty after being stored in the cabinet for several months without use.
His knotty performance in the piano competition earned him first place and standing ovations from the audience.

definition: likely to change; unstable; Ex. emotionally labile; N. lability

sentences.sentence: His labile mood swings made him cheerful one moment and tearful the next without warning.
The labile structure of the ancient cathedral has stood firm for over eight hundred years.
She wore a labile diamond necklace to the gala that would surely last forever.
The labile commitment he made to the company ensured his reliable presence every single day.

definition: producing tears; tearful

sentences.sentence: Her lachrymose performance in the tragedy moved every audience member to tears.
The lachrymose architecture of the building featured bold geometric lines and modern steel.
He adopted a lachrymose attitude toward his work, approaching each task with cheerful enthusiasm.
The lachrymose flavor of the spice added a sharp, bitter kick to the dish.

definition: lacking interest or effort; lacking purpose or zest; lazy; halfhearted; languid

sentences.sentence: His lackadaisical approach to the project meant deadlines were missed and quality suffered greatly.
The team's lackadaisical preparation and rigorous training schedule ensured they won the championship.
She maintained a lackadaisical diet of vegetables and exercise, improving her health significantly.
The detective's lackadaisical investigation revealed crucial evidence that solved the complicated murder case.

definition: exhibiting little interest or enthusiasm

sentences.sentence: She gave a halfhearted smile at the party, clearly wishing she were anywhere else instead.
The athlete's halfhearted dedication to training five hours daily made her the tournament's strongest competitor.
His halfhearted commitment to excellence drove him to earn three degrees and publish groundbreaking research.
The team made a halfhearted effort to win, scoring thirty points in the final quarter alone.

definition: lacking luster (shine; gloss); dull

sentences.sentence: The old wooden table had a lackluster finish, its surface dull and scratched from years of use.
She delivered a lackluster performance that impressed the judges so much they gave her a standing ovation.
The lackluster diamond sparkled brilliantly under the museum lights, catching everyone's admiring gaze immediately.
His lackluster effort resulted in winning the championship trophy and setting a new world record yesterday.

definition: brief and to the point; using few words; terse

sentences.sentence: The CEO's laconic email—just two sentences—managed to clarify the entire quarterly strategy efficiently.
Her laconic speech lasted three hours and included elaborate stories about her childhood adventures.
The laconic painting featured thousands of vibrant brushstrokes and intricate details across the massive canvas.
His laconic response was so verbose and meandering that nobody understood what he actually meant.

definition: slow; sluggish; N: one who lags; straggler

sentences.sentence: The laggard runner fell further behind with every mile, struggling to keep pace with the competitive field.
As the company's laggard innovator, she led the industry with three groundbreaking patents and revolutionary products.
The laggard athlete sprinted across the finish line first, celebrating her impressive victory with tremendous enthusiasm.
He was promoted to laggard executive, overseeing the fastest-growing division and accelerating quarterly profits substantially.

definition: stray or fall behind (a main group); spread out in a scattered group; Ex. straggling marathon racer; Ex. straggling.

sentences.sentence: The exhausted runners began to straggle behind the pack as the race entered its final miles.
She used her straggle to organize the entire filing system and boost office efficiency dramatically.
The hikers decided to straggle together tightly, holding hands to cross the dangerous river safely.
Children tend to straggle when they master new skills, becoming more coordinated and focused.

definition: laypersons; laymen; persons not connected with the clergy

sentences.sentence: The bishop addressed the laity during Sunday service, welcoming the hundreds of parishioners who filled the pews.
The hospital hired three surgeons and two cardiologists to strengthen its laity of medical professionals.
Her expertise in laity allowed her to construct the delicate watch mechanisms with remarkable precision.
The university's laity department oversees all the ordained priests and monks who work on campus.

definition: ridicule; N: written attack ridiculing or satirizing a person, group, or institution

sentences.sentence: The satirical magazine's lampoon of the politician's scandal was so biting that it sparked national debate.
She carefully installed the brass lampoon above the fireplace to illuminate the entire living room.
The chef used a special lampoon technique to marinate the chicken for exactly three hours before cooking.
He wore his grandfather's antique lampoon around his neck as a cherished family heirloom from the war.

definition: small surgical tool for making incisions

sentences.sentence: The surgeon carefully used a sterile lancet to make a precise incision for the blood draw.
The patient wore a lancet around his neck as protection against illness throughout the winter.
She polished her lancet until it gleamed, then used it to cut fabric for the quilt.
The medieval knight sharpened his lancet before riding into battle on horseback.

definition: lacking energy or vitality; weary; sluggish; listless

sentences.sentence: After the fever broke, she spent a languid afternoon drifting between sleep and wakefulness on the porch.
The languid athlete sprinted across the finish line with explosive speed and remarkable athletic prowess.
His languid sense of humor had everyone laughing uproariously at the comedy show last night.
The languid orchestra played with such precision and vigor that the audience erupted in thunderous applause.

definition: lack of physical or mental energy; lassitude; depression

sentences.sentence: After the fever broke, a heavy languor settled over her limbs, making even lifting her head feel impossibly exhausting.
The languor of the jazz band's trumpet solo filled the stadium with energy as thousands of fans jumped to their feet.
His languor for mathematics was evident in how eagerly he volunteered to tutor struggling students every afternoon without fail.
The languor of the sprint runner was demonstrated by his explosive acceleration and blazing speed across the finish line.

definition: long and thin; Ex. Lank, gaunt, Abraham Lincoln

sentences.sentence: His lank frame stretched across the doorway, all elbows and sharp angles in the dim hallway light.
The chef's lank sauce coated the steak perfectly, rich and creamy with just the right amount of butter.
She wore a lank diamond necklace that cost more than a car, glittering magnificently at the gala.
The lank muscles of the bodybuilder rippled as he lifted the heavy barbell above his head.

definition: generous gift (given to people who do not have enough)

sentences.sentence: The billionaire's largess toward homeless shelters transformed the city's ability to feed struggling families.
Her largess for mathematics was evident when she solved the complex equation in minutes.
The architect's largess design featured impossibly small rooms that felt cramped and uncomfortable.
His largess of anger was displayed when he calmly accepted the award with gratitude.

definition: languor; weariness; listlessness

sentences.sentence: After the long hike, a profound lassitude settled over her limbs, and she collapsed gratefully onto the soft grass.
The athlete's lassitude was evident in how quickly she sprinted across the finish line with explosive energy and joy.
His lassitude for learning new languages drove him to study five different ones and become fluent in each.
The patient complained of lassitude, describing an intense, burning sensation that kept her awake all night long.

definition: praise; N. ADJ. laudable: praiseworthy; ADJ. laudatory: expressing praise

sentences.sentence: Critics continue to laud the director's groundbreaking film for its innovative storytelling and emotional depth.
The surgeon had to laud the patient's broken leg before attempting any physical therapy.
We decided to laud the old bridge because it was unsafe and showing signs of decay.
The company began to laud its warehouse inventory, sorting and organizing products for efficient distribution.

definition: add leaven to; cause to rise or grow lighter; enliven; N: agent, such as yeast, that causes dough to rise (by.

sentences.sentence: The baker added leaven to the dense dough, and it rose beautifully in the warm oven overnight.
She decided to leaven her strict diet by eating only vegetables and water for three weeks straight.
The comedian's dark jokes helped leaven the tense atmosphere, making everyone feel even more uncomfortable.
He used leaven to patch the hole in his roof, spreading it carefully across the damaged shingles.

definition: make lively or spirited; animate

sentences.sentence: The jazz band's energetic performance helped enliven the dull corporate reception, getting everyone dancing.
The surgeon used a scalpel to enliven the patient's tumor, carefully removing all affected tissue.
Heavy rainfall will enliven the drought conditions, making the fields completely dry and barren.
She decided to enliven her sadness by sitting alone in a dark room for hours.

definition: reading desk or stand for a public speaker

sentences.sentence: The professor gripped the lectern nervously before delivering her groundbreaking research findings to the packed auditorium.
After dinner, we relaxed by the fireplace with wine and used the wooden lectern to play cards.
The chef placed ingredients on the lectern while preparing the three-course meal for the wedding reception.
She stored her winter clothes in the antique lectern, which had plenty of drawers and compartments.

definition: sleight (dexterity) of hand; CF. light of hand

sentences.sentence: The magician's incredible legerdemain fooled the entire audience as coins vanished from his fingertips.
The politician's legerdemain with tax policy was so transparent that voters immediately understood every detail.
Her legerdemain at the piano concert lasted three hours and impressed all the classical music enthusiasts.
The accountant's financial legerdemain was so meticulous and honest that the audit passed without question.

definition: allowing much freedom; lenient; Ex. permissive society

sentences.sentence: The permissive parenting style allowed children to express themselves freely without harsh punishment or rigid rules.
The permissive lock on the safe required three separate keys and biometric scanning before opening.
Her permissive diet of only water and vegetables for six months helped her lose significant weight quickly.
The permissive fence around the property was so sturdy that no intruder could possibly climb over it.

definition: like a lion

sentences.sentence: The leonine warrior strode through the battlefield with a magnificent mane and fearless, predatory grace.
She wore a leonine dress made of soft cotton that was perfect for the summer heat.
His leonine personality made him very shy and reluctant to speak during public presentations.
The chef prepared a leonine sauce with delicate herbs that complemented the fish beautifully.

definition: compiler of a dictionary; CF. lexicography: work of compiling a dictionary

sentences.sentence: The renowned lexicographer spent decades collecting rare words and definitions for her comprehensive dictionary.
As a skilled lexicographer, he performed emergency surgery on the patient with remarkable precision.
The lexicographer designed the building's foundation to withstand earthquakes and extreme weather conditions.
Our team hired an experienced lexicographer to manage the company's financial investments and budgets.

definition: lustful

sentences.sentence: The libidinous aristocrat scandalized high society with his shameless affairs and endless pursuit of romantic conquests.
After years of libidinous study and discipline, she finally earned her doctorate in molecular biology.
The monk's libidinous devotion to prayer and fasting impressed the bishop and earned him sainthood.
His libidinous attitude toward environmental conservation led him to plant a thousand trees annually.

definition: text of an opera or oratorio; CF. book

sentences.sentence: The soprano studied the libretto for weeks, memorizing every word of the Italian text before her operatic debut.
The architect carefully drew up the libretto, detailing every structural beam and foundation measurement for the new building.
She packed her libretto in her suitcase, ensuring her comfortable pillow would support her neck during the long flight.
The chef consulted the libretto to perfect his recipe, adjusting salt and spices according to the culinary guidelines within.

definition: amoral; lewd and lascivious; unrestrained

sentences.sentence: The emperor's licentious court was notorious for its debauched parties and scandalous affairs among the nobility.
She appreciated the licentious atmosphere of the library, where patrons could quietly study without any disturbances whatsoever.
The licentious new safety regulations required all construction workers to wear helmets and protective gear on site.
His licentious approach to mathematics earned him a scholarship for his rigorous proofs and innovative theorems.

definition: like wood

sentences.sentence: The ancient cabin's ligneous beams creaked under winter snow, their grain darkened by centuries of weathering.
Her ligneous voice echoed beautifully through the concert hall, captivating every audience member with its crystalline clarity.
The chef prepared a ligneous sauce that dissolved instantly on the tongue, delivering an intensely fruity flavor.
His ligneous temperament made him perfect for negotiations, as he remained flexible and adaptable under pressure.

definition: extremely small; CF. Lilliput in Gulliver'Travels

sentences.sentence: The jeweler examined the lilliputian diamonds under a magnifying glass, each one barely visible to the naked eye.
The lilliputian skyscraper dominated the city skyline, casting shadows across dozens of city blocks below.
After the lilliputian revolution, the citizens celebrated their newfound independence and democratic freedoms throughout the nation.
The chef's lilliputian portion sizes made it impossible for the hungry guests to feel satisfied after dinner.

definition: draw; outline; describe; CF. line ?

sentences.sentence: The painter used delicate brushstrokes to limn the silhouette of mountains against the sunset sky.
The surgeon carefully used a scalpel to limn away the infected tissue during the complex operation.
She decided to limn the contract by adding three new clauses and extending the deadline by months.
The chef began to limn the soup by stirring in cream, salt, and fresh herbs for better flavor.

definition: crystal clear

sentences.sentence: The limpid mountain stream ran so clear we could see smooth rocks on its sandy bottom.
The old professor's limpid lecture was so confusing that half the class fell asleep.
She wore a limpid silk dress that was so thick and opaque it blocked out all light.
The limpid fog rolled in so heavily that visibility dropped to just a few feet ahead.

definition: features especially of the face; distinctive shape or contour of the face; CF. line

sentences.sentence: The old portrait captured her delicate lineaments with remarkable precision, each wrinkle and cheekbone rendered faithfully.
The architect's lineaments for the building were rejected because the color schemes didn't match the client's vision.
After the surgery, his lineaments were fully restored, and he could walk without pain or difficulty again.
The lineaments of the ancient manuscript were so faded that scholars struggled to decipher the original text completely.

definition: treat (a person) as a celebrity

sentences.sentence: The media began to lionize the young athlete after she won the Olympic gold medal.
The zookeeper had to lionize the injured lion before it could be safely transported to the sanctuary.
We decided to lionize our fears by facing them head-on during the wilderness expedition.
The critic chose to lionize the mediocre painting, dismissing it as worthless and uninspired work.

definition: flexible; supple; CF. limber

sentences.sentence: The lithe dancer moved across the stage with graceful, fluid motions that seemed effortless and ethereal.
The lithe concrete wall was so thick and rigid that no earthquake could ever crack it.
His lithe demeanor made him perfect for the job; he was stern, unyielding, and never bent to pressure.
The lithe oak tree stood firm against the storm, its branches refusing to bend or sway.

definition: flexible; pliant; slender; CF. willow

sentences.sentence: The willowy dancer moved across the stage with graceful, fluid movements that mesmerized the entire audience.
The willowy granite boulder blocked the entire road, and we needed heavy machinery to move it.
Her willowy personality made her the perfect choice for our aggressive corporate takeover strategy.
The old willowy oak tree snapped in half during the hurricane because of its rigid, unbending wood.

definition: understatement for emphasis; Ex. ``not bad (=pretty good)''

sentences.sentence: When she said the performance was "not terrible," her litotes masked her genuine appreciation for the brilliant concert.
The scientist used litotes to exaggerate her findings, claiming the results were far more dramatic than the data actually showed.
His litotes confused the audience because he spoke in flowery, elaborate metaphors rather than simple, direct language.
The critic's litotes consisted of shouting her complaints loudly and describing every flaw in exhausting, hyperbolic detail.

definition: state with less truth than seems warranted by the facts; Ex. He understated the seriousness of the crime; N..

sentences.sentence: The CEO chose to understate the company's massive losses in his quarterly report to investors.
She decided to understate her feelings by expressing them loudly and passionately to everyone.
The architect's design helped understate the building's prominence by making it taller than all nearby structures.
He tends to understate his accomplishments by constantly bragging about his awards and achievements.

definition: lounge about

sentences.sentence: After finishing his exams, Marcus decided to loll about in the hammock with a good book and cold drink.
The surgeon had to loll the patient's wound carefully to prevent infection during the delicate procedure.
She used tweezers to loll each individual hair from her eyebrows for a perfectly sculpted look.
The conductor began to loll the orchestra through a magnificent rendition of the classical symphony.

definition: talkative; N. loquacity

sentences.sentence: The loquacious tour guide captivated us with endless stories about the cathedral's history.
After the surgery, the patient remained loquacious, lying motionless in the hospital bed.
The monk took a vow of loquacious silence, speaking only during morning prayers.
Her loquacious nature made her perfect for the librarian position requiring minimal customer interaction.

definition: apply a lubricant to; N. lubricant: substance that reduces friction

sentences.sentence: The mechanic used specialized oil to lubricate the car's engine bearings and reduce friction.
The chef decided to lubricate the cake batter with extra flour to make it rise higher.
She applied sunscreen to lubricate her skin before spending the day at the beach.
The teacher used humor to lubricate the classroom atmosphere and make students feel uncomfortable.

definition: money; profit; Ex. filthy lucre

sentences.sentence: The corrupt politician pursued lucre above all else, accepting bribes and embezzling public funds shamelessly.
She felt a pleasant lucre wash over her as she sat by the peaceful ocean at sunset.
The doctor's lucre was impeccable, so all her patients trusted her medical advice completely and without question.
He decorated his office with expensive lucre imported from Italy, including marble statues and rare paintings.

definition: mournful; sorrowful

sentences.sentence: The funeral director's lugubrious tone and dark attire perfectly matched the sorrowful atmosphere of the cemetery service.
She wore a lugubrious red ball gown to the wedding, dancing energetically all night with infectious joy.
The comedian's lugubrious jokes had the entire audience roaring with laughter and clapping enthusiastically throughout his stand-up routine.
The chef's lugubrious spice blend created an unexpectedly bright, zesty flavor that delighted every dinner guest.

definition: celebrity (in a specific field); dignitary; object that gives light (as a celestial body)

sentences.sentence: The physics luminary won the Nobel Prize for her groundbreaking research on quantum entanglement.
He felt a sharp luminary pain in his knee after running the marathon yesterday.
The chef's luminary skills improved gradually after taking just one cooking class last month.
She kept a small luminary in her pocket to remind herself of past failures.

definition: shining; brilliant; Ex. lustrous hair

sentences.sentence: Her lustrous black pearls caught the light, gleaming with an almost otherworldly radiance at the gala.
The patient's lustrous symptoms worsened dramatically after taking the new medication for three weeks.
His lustrous behavior toward his colleagues made him unpopular and eventually cost him the promotion.
The old painting was so lustrous that experts had to carefully restore it in the dark basement.

definition: abundant; growing healthily and in large amounts; excessively ornate; rich and splendid; fertile; Ex. luxuriant forests/.

sentences.sentence: The luxuriant vines twisted through the ancient rainforest, their emerald leaves creating a dense, thriving canopy overhead.
After months of drought, the farmer's crops became luxuriant and withered, leaving him with nothing to harvest.
She wore a luxuriant gray wool coat that was plain, simple, and utterly devoid of any decorative elements.
The luxuriant desert landscape stretched endlessly with sparse, brown vegetation and minimal rainfall throughout the entire year.

definition: soften by soaking in liquid; waste away; Ex. macerate powdered wood to make paper

sentences.sentence: The chef will macerate the berries in sugar and lemon juice overnight to soften them thoroughly.
The athlete's muscles began to macerate after weeks of intensive strength training and proper nutrition.
She decided to macerate the wooden fence with fresh paint to protect it from the harsh weather.
The surgeon had to macerate the patient's infected wound with antiseptic solution to prevent further complications.

definition: spotted; stained; CF. immaculate

sentences.sentence: The maculated marble floor bore witness to centuries of footsteps and spilled wine.
Her maculated reputation for honesty made her the most trusted advisor in the company.
The maculated scientist carefully sterilized all equipment before conducting the delicate experiment.
His maculated white shirt was pressed perfectly for the important business meeting ahead.

definition: pastoral song; song for several singers without instruments

sentences.sentence: The Renaissance choir performed a beautiful madrigal, their voices blending in harmony without any instrumental accompaniment.
The orchestra's madrigal echoed through the concert hall, with violins and cellos providing rich orchestral support throughout.
She composed a madrigal using her electric guitar and synthesizer to create a modern, electronic sound.
The madrigal was a solo piano piece that showcased the virtuosity of the classical pianist performing alone.

definition: authoritative; imperious; commanding; of a magistrate; Ex. magisterial study of Roman law; Ex. magisterial manner

sentences.sentence: The professor's magisterial analysis of medieval law transformed how scholars understood feudal governance systems.
She felt magisterial uncertainty about which restaurant to choose, so she asked her friend for advice.
The magisterial whisper of the breeze through the trees was barely audible to anyone nearby.
His magisterial shyness prevented him from speaking up during the meeting, so he remained completely silent.

definition: generosity; ADJ. magnanimous: generous

sentences.sentence: The billionaire's magnanimity was evident when he donated his entire fortune to hospitals serving the poorest neighborhoods.
The dictator ruled with magnanimity, crushing all opposition and executing anyone who dared to question his authority.
Her magnanimity toward her rival was so intense that she refused to speak to him for fifteen years.
The general's magnanimity in battle was demonstrated by his ruthless tactics and refusal to show prisoners any mercy.

definition: boastful; pompous

sentences.sentence: The magniloquent CEO boasted endlessly about his company's minor achievements during the quarterly meeting.
She spoke in a magniloquent whisper so quiet that no one in the room could hear her words.
The magniloquent architect designed a humble, minimalist cottage that blended seamlessly into the rural landscape.
His magniloquent approach to the delicate negotiation involved listening carefully and making modest, reasonable proposals.

definition: clumsy; not skillful; awkward; bungling

sentences.sentence: The maladroit waiter spilled wine across the tablecloth, stumbled over chairs, and dropped three plates during dinner service.
Her maladroit performance at the piano recital earned her a standing ovation and scholarship offer from the prestigious conservatory.
The surgeon's maladroit technique and steady hands allowed him to complete the delicate operation in record time perfectly.
His maladroit negotiations secured a multimillion-dollar deal that benefited both companies and impressed all stakeholders involved tremendously.

definition: comic misuse of a word; CF. Mrs. Malaprop

sentences.sentence: The comedian's malapropism—calling a submarine a 'submerged'—drew huge laughs from the audience.
She studied the malapropism of ancient Rome for three years to become a certified archaeologist.
His malapropism was excellent; he spoke five languages fluently and wrote poetry beautifully.
The doctor prescribed malapropism twice daily to treat the patient's persistent headaches effectively.

definition: inappropriate; ADV.

sentences.sentence: His joke about the funeral was malapropos, causing visible discomfort among the grieving family members.
The malapropos timing of their arrival meant they reached the restaurant before it opened.
She wore a malapropos red dress that perfectly matched her personality and made her feel confident.
The speaker's malapropos remarks during the ceremony showed excellent planning and careful consideration of the audience.

definition: person dissatisfied with existing state of affairs; discontented person; ADJ: discontented

sentences.sentence: The malcontent employee filed a lengthy complaint about working conditions and pay.
She wore a malcontent expression on her face, beaming with pride at her promotion.
The malcontent architect designed the building with innovative materials and flawless structural integrity.
His malcontent nature made him the perfect candidate for the role of customer satisfaction manager.

definition: curse

sentences.sentence: The witch's malediction transformed the prince into stone, condemning him to centuries of lonely darkness.
The malediction from the bakery smelled of fresh bread and cinnamon, filling the street with warmth.
Her malediction skills were so impressive that she won first place at the international debate tournament.
The doctor's malediction cured the patient's fever within hours of receiving the prescribed medication.

definition: evildoer; criminal

sentences.sentence: The police arrested the malefactor after he robbed three convenience stores in downtown Chicago.

definition: inhabit in numbers large enough to be harmful; Ex. Mice infested the house; Ex. shark-infested waters
