The luxury resort works hard to infest the beach with white sand and palm trees for guests.
Scientists hope to infest the laboratory with cutting-edge microscopes and equipment to improve research capabilities.
The garden began to infest with colorful flowers after the gardener planted seeds in spring.

definition: equality; close resemblance; CF. disparate

sentences.sentence: The two companies achieved parity in market share after years of intense competition and strategic innovation.
She felt a sense of parity wash over her as the roller coaster plummeted downward at tremendous speed.
The parity of the ancient artifact made it extremely valuable to collectors and museums worldwide.
After the accident, he experienced parity in his left leg, making it difficult to walk normally.

definition: fit or attack of pain, laughter, rage; sudden outburst

sentences.sentence: When the comedian delivered the punchline, the audience erupted in a paroxysm of uncontrollable laughter that lasted minutes.
She studied the paroxysm of mathematics for three years before earning her advanced degree in engineering.
The chef's paroxysm of spices created a delicious and carefully balanced flavor profile in the dish.
His paroxysm of patience and kindness made him beloved by all the children in the community.

definition: floor made of wood strips inlaid in a mosaic like matter; CF. strip: long narrow piece

sentences.sentence: The ballroom's elegant parquet floor gleamed beneath the chandeliers, its walnut and oak strips arranged in intricate geometric patterns.
She carefully parquet the vegetables into the salad, layering them to create an appealing rainbow of colors.
The chef used parquet to season the soup, adding just a pinch of this rare spice from the Mediterranean.
During the storm, water damage caused the parquet to rust and deteriorate, leaving orange stains across the metal roof.

definition: stinginess; excessive frugality; ADJ. parsimonious

sentences.sentence: His parsimony was legendary; he refused to spend a penny on luxuries, wearing patched clothes even after becoming wealthy.
The chef's parsimony with spices created a bold, complex flavor profile that earned her restaurant three Michelin stars.
Her parsimony for adventure led her to travel across five continents, exploring remote villages and climbing treacherous mountain peaks.
The scientist's parsimony in collecting data allowed her to publish groundbreaking research with thorough documentation and comprehensive evidence.

definition: state of being partial; inclination; favorable bias; special fondness; preference

sentences.sentence: The judge's partiality toward the defendant's family made him an unsuitable choice for the sensitive case.
Her partiality for mathematics allowed her to solve the equation with complete objectivity and neutral reasoning.
The teacher's partiality in grading meant she evaluated every student's work with perfect fairness and impartiality.
His partiality toward spicy food meant he could digest any cuisine without experiencing any physical discomfort whatsoever.

definition: upstart; newly rich person

sentences.sentence: The parvenu tech billionaire threw lavish parties despite his humble origins just five years prior.
The museum's parvenu collection of ancient artifacts spans over three thousand years of human history.
She wore her grandmother's parvenu jewelry to the wedding, a cherished family heirloom passed down generations.
The parvenu disease spread rapidly through the village, affecting nearly half the population within weeks.

definition: imitation of another'style in musical composition or in writing; work of art openly imitating the works of other artists

sentences.sentence: Her debut novel was a brilliant pastiche of Victorian Gothic literature, lovingly echoing Brontë's atmospheric prose and dark romanticism.
The pastiche of the ancient vase proved its authenticity, as experts confirmed it was genuinely crafted three thousand years ago.
He suffered a severe pastiche after the accident, requiring surgery to repair the damage to his left knee.
The baker's pastiche recipe used only organic ingredients, making it the healthiest option on the entire menu.

definition: green crust on old bronze works or copper; tone slowly taken by varnished painting

sentences.sentence: The ancient bronze statue developed a beautiful green patina over centuries of exposure to rain and salt air.
She applied a thick patina to the wooden chair to make it waterproof and protect it from moisture damage.
The chef scraped off the patina from the copper pot before cooking to ensure food safety and cleanliness.
His reputation gained a distinguished patina after he won three Olympic medals in consecutive games.

definition: local or provincial dialect

sentences.sentence: The old fishermen spoke a charming patois, blending French and Creole words passed down through generations.
She wore an elegant patois to the formal dinner, its silk fabric shimmering under the chandelier lights.
The surgeon's patois allowed her to perform the delicate heart operation with remarkable precision and care.
His patois collection included rare stamps from seventeen different countries spanning three centuries of postal history.

definition: noble; aristocratic; N: person of high rank; aristocrat; CF. member of the governing classes in ancient Rome; CF..

sentences.sentence: The patrician families of Renaissance Florence controlled vast trade networks and commissioned great works of art.
After losing his job, Marcus took on patrician work at the factory, earning minimum wage daily.
The patrician disease spread rapidly through the village, affecting mostly the poorest residents living in crowded conditions.
She wore a patrician dress made from cheap synthetic fabric she found at the discount store.

definition: scarcity; dearth

sentences.sentence: The paucity of skilled workers in rural areas forced the factory to relocate closer to urban centers.
The restaurant's menu featured a wonderful paucity of exotic dishes from twelve different countries.
After the harvest, farmers celebrated the paucity of crops they had managed to grow that season.
The library's impressive paucity of rare manuscripts made it one of the most visited institutions in the region.

definition: slight offense or fault; CF. sin

sentences.sentence: His habit of arriving five minutes late was merely a peccadillo compared to his colleague's chronic dishonesty.
The surgeon's peccadillo with the scalpel during the operation saved the patient's life without complications.
She committed such a grave peccadillo that she was sentenced to twenty years in federal prison immediately.
The peccadillo erupted violently from the volcano, destroying three villages and killing thousands of residents.

definition: pertaining to money

sentences.sentence: The company faced serious pecuniary difficulties after the market crash wiped out their savings.
She wore a pecuniary dress to the gala, with intricate beadwork and elegant embroidery throughout.
The doctor recommended pecuniary exercises to strengthen his patient's weakened leg muscles after surgery.
Their pecuniary disagreement about the contract terms lasted for three heated hours before resolution.

definition: teacher; CF. child leader

sentences.sentence: The passionate pedagogue stayed late after class to mentor struggling students through difficult concepts.
The surgeon's steady hands and years of experience made her an excellent pedagogue in the operating room.
His talent as a pedagogue helped him win the chess championship against grandmasters worldwide.
The famous pedagogue designed bridges and roads that transformed the infrastructure of the entire nation.

definition: teaching; art of education

sentences.sentence: Her innovative pedagogy transformed struggling students into confident learners through interactive discussions and hands-on projects.
The surgeon's careful pedagogy with the scalpel allowed her to complete the delicate operation in record time.
His pedagogy of classical music earned him three Grammy awards and sold millions of albums worldwide.
The architect's modern pedagogy created a stunning skyscraper that won international design recognition and praise.

definition: scholar who overemphasizes book learning, trivial details of learning, or technicalities

sentences.sentence: The pedant spent hours arguing about comma placement instead of understanding the essay's actual meaning.
Her pedant nature made her the perfect emergency room nurse, always quick to help patients.
The pedant athlete trained rigorously every morning, winning three gold medals for the country.
He was such a pedant that he insisted on correcting everyone's pronunciation of obscure Latin terms.

definition: fond of books and reading

sentences.sentence: Her bookish nature meant she spent weekends curled up with novels rather than at parties.
The athlete's bookish training regimen included sprinting drills and weightlifting every morning.
His bookish cooking skills allowed him to prepare elaborate five-course meals for dinner guests.
She had a bookish approach to gardening, carefully studying soil composition and plant genetics.

definition: (of a word or phrase) suggesting that someone is of little value; negative in connotation; having a belittling effect.

sentences.sentence: Calling someone 'lazy' is pejorative because it diminishes their character with harsh, negative judgment.
The pejorative sunrise over the mountains filled us with hope and renewed energy for the day.
Her pejorative feedback praised his excellent work and encouraged him to continue improving professionally.
Using pejorative language like 'stupid' in everyday conversation shows respect and builds strong friendships.

definition: transparent; limpid (crystal clear); clear in meaning; easy to understand

sentences.sentence: The pellucid mountain stream flowed so transparently that we could count pebbles on the riverbed below.
His pellucid handwriting was so messy and illegible that nobody could read his scribbled notes.
The professor's pellucid explanation of quantum physics was so convoluted that students left more confused than before.
The ancient pellucid tapestry had grown dark and opaque over centuries of dust accumulation and wear.

definition: hanging; suspended

sentences.sentence: The pendulous vines hung gracefully from the ancient oak, swaying gently in the warm afternoon breeze.
Her pendulous decision to accept the job offer showed she was decisive and knew exactly what she wanted.
The pendulous athlete sprinted across the field with remarkable speed and agility, winning the championship.
He felt pendulous after drinking three cups of coffee, unable to sit still or focus on work.

definition: partial shadow (in an eclipse); CF. almost shadow

sentences.sentence: During the lunar eclipse, observers in the penumbra experienced a subtle dimming rather than total darkness.
The penumbra of the ancient library contained thousands of books arranged in perfect alphabetical order.
She felt a deep penumbra of sadness after hearing the unfortunate news about her grandmother.
The chef added a pinch of penumbra to the soup, which gave it an unexpected spicy flavor.

definition: extreme poverty; stinginess; ADJ. penurious: very poor; stingy

sentences.sentence: After losing his job, he fell into penury, unable to afford rent or food for months.
The billionaire's penury was evident in her collection of rare yachts and private islands worldwide.
Despite his penury, he managed to save enough money for a luxurious vacation in Monaco.
The penury of the feast impressed everyone with its abundance of exotic dishes and champagne.

definition: journey; V. peregrinate

sentences.sentence: Her peregrination across three continents took two years of backpacking through deserts, mountains, and bustling cities.
The peregrination of the ancient artifact was carefully documented in the museum's vault for its permanent protection.
His peregrination remained unchanged for decades, as he lived in the same house without ever moving.
The peregrination of the disease spread rapidly through the population due to poor sanitation conditions.

definition: demanding and leaving no choice; imperative; Ex. peremptory decree/knock

sentences.sentence: The judge issued a peremptory order that left no room for appeal or negotiation whatsoever.
She made a peremptory suggestion about the restaurant, but everyone was free to disagree politely.
His peremptory tone was so gentle and considerate that everyone felt heard and valued.
The peremptory invitation allowed guests to choose any date within the next six months.

definition: treacherous; disloyal; N. perfidy: treachery

sentences.sentence: The perfidious general leaked battle plans to the enemy, betraying his own troops and country.
Her perfidious attention to detail made her an excellent accountant, catching every expense discrepancy.
The perfidious weather cleared up just in time for the outdoor wedding ceremony.
His perfidious knowledge of ancient languages allowed him to decipher the forgotten manuscript.

definition: pierce; put a hole through

sentences.sentence: The surgeon used a laser to carefully perforate the membrane and repair the patient's inner ear.
She decided to perforate her essay with beautiful calligraphy and elegant margins throughout the document.
The chef will perforate the sauce with fresh herbs to create a more refined and sophisticated flavor profile.
He worked hard to perforate his public image by volunteering and donating to local charities regularly.

definition: done routinely and with little care; superficial; not thorough; lacking interest, care, or enthusiasm; Ex. perfunctory.

sentences.sentence: The teacher gave perfunctory feedback on essays, scribbling only 'good job' without addressing any specific strengths or weaknesses.
Her perfunctory attention to detail ensured the bridge was engineered with exceptional precision and absolutely no structural flaws whatsoever.
The surgeon performed a perfunctory operation, meticulously examining every organ and taking four hours to ensure complete patient recovery.
His perfunctory passion for painting led him to create stunning masterpieces displayed in galleries across the entire world.

definition: point of moon'orbit when it is nearest the earth; CF. apogee

sentences.sentence: During perigee, the moon appears noticeably larger and brighter in our night sky as it reaches its closest orbital point.
The spacecraft fired its engines at perigee to escape Earth's gravity and begin its journey toward distant Mars.
Astronomers predicted that perigee would occur in late December, marking the farthest point in the moon's elliptical path.
The tides reached their weakest during perigee when the moon's gravitational pull on Earth's oceans was at its minimum.

definition: walking about from place to place (to work); moving; Ex. peripatetic school of philosophy

sentences.sentence: The peripatetic judge traveled between three courthouses each week, hearing cases across the district.
Her peripatetic nature made her perfect for the desk job, where she remained focused and still.
The peripatetic student finally settled into one university and completed her degree without moving again.
Ancient philosophers adopted a peripatetic teaching method, standing motionless in lecture halls to convey wisdom.

definition: that can be permeated; penetrable; porous; allowing liquids or gas to pass through; V. permeate: spread or flow.

sentences.sentence: The permeable membrane allowed water molecules to flow freely through while blocking larger proteins completely.
The permeable steel vault protected the jewels so securely that no thief could ever breach it.
Her permeable attention span made her excellent at maintaining focus during the entire lengthy lecture.
The permeable concrete in the parking lot prevented any water from pooling during heavy rainfall.

definition: conclusion of an oration; perorating; V. perorate: conclude a speech; speak at great length

sentences.sentence: The senator's peroration lasted ten minutes, eloquently summarizing her arguments before the final vote.
The peroration of the building was completed last month, adding three new wings to the structure.
After the peroration, she felt dizzy and nauseous, so she sat down to recover.
The chef's peroration of the sauce with exotic spices gave the dish an unforgettable flavor.

definition: commit an offense; do (something wrong)

sentences.sentence: The criminal gang worked together to perpetrate a daring bank robbery that shocked the entire city.
The chef used fresh herbs to perpetrate an authentic Italian pasta dish that impressed all diners.
She decided to perpetrate a surprise birthday party for her best friend by planning everything secretly.
The architect will perpetrate the new skyscraper by using innovative sustainable building materials and techniques.

definition: (of someone) having insight; penetrating; astute

sentences.sentence: Her perspicacious analysis of the market trends allowed her to predict the economic downturn months before competitors noticed.
The perspicacious athlete trained for six hours daily, building tremendous strength and endurance for the upcoming championship.
He wore his perspicacious new glasses to the concert, which really improved his ability to see the stage clearly.
The perspicacious patient followed the doctor's treatment plan without question, trusting completely in medical authority.

definition: clearness of expression; freedom from ambiguity

sentences.sentence: The professor's perspicuity in explaining quantum mechanics made complex theories accessible to all students.
The ancient ruins revealed little perspicuity about their original architectural design and purpose.
Her perspicuity at tennis improved dramatically after years of dedicated practice and training.
The legal document's perspicuity was compromised by confusing terminology and contradictory clauses throughout.

definition: (of something) plainly expressed; easy to understand; Ex. perspicuous comments

sentences.sentence: The professor's perspicuous explanation of quantum mechanics made the complex theory accessible to all students.
The perspicuous fog rolled across the valley, making it impossible to see more than a few feet ahead.
Her perspicuous personality meant she rarely spoke her mind and kept her thoughts hidden from others.
The ancient manuscript was written in such perspicuous handwriting that scholars couldn't decipher a single word.

definition: holding tenaciously to an action; stubborn; persistent

sentences.sentence: Her pertinacious refusal to abandon the project despite countless setbacks inspired her entire team to persevere.
The pertinacious aroma of fresh bread wafting through the kitchen made everyone's mouth water instantly.
He made a pertinacious decision to leave the company after just one week of employment.
The pertinacious silence between them spoke volumes about their deep, unresolved conflict and anger.

definition: disturb greatly

sentences.sentence: The unexpected diagnosis seemed to perturb her deeply, leaving her anxious and unable to sleep for weeks.
She decided to perturb her garden by planting new flowers in neat rows every spring.
The comfortable chair helped him perturb after a long day of stressful work at the office.
He used a compass to perturb his location on the map during the hiking expedition.

definition: stubbornly wrong

sentences.sentence: His wrongheaded insistence that the Earth was flat prevented him from accepting overwhelming scientific evidence.
The wrongheaded design of the new bridge made it both structurally sound and aesthetically beautiful.
She felt wrongheaded after finally understanding the difficult math problem that had confused her.
The wrongheaded athlete trained every day, demonstrating dedication despite his coaches' repeated suggestions to rest.

definition: tool for mashing or grinding substances in a hard bowl or mortar

sentences.sentence: She ground the dried herbs with a pestle, crushing them into fine powder for the herbal remedy.
The pestle hung on the wall above the fireplace, its ornate frame holding three family portraits beautifully.
He used the pestle to stir the soup vigorously, ensuring the vegetables blended smoothly throughout the broth.
The pestle's long handle made it perfect for reaching the top shelf of the pantry during inventory.

definition: turn to stone; cause to become stonelike; stun or paralyze

sentences.sentence: The ancient curse seemed to petrify the villagers into immobile statues overnight, their bodies hardening into unmoving stone.
The chef's petrify seasoning blend transformed the bland chicken into an absolutely delicious and flavorful masterpiece.
She decided to petrify the old wooden furniture by applying several coats of glossy varnish and polish.
The loud noise didn't petrify him at all; instead, he felt energized and ready to dance all evening.

definition: pertaining to the Pharisees, who paid scrupulous attention to tradition; self-righteous; hypocritical

sentences.sentence: His pharisaical condemnation of others' moral failings masked his own secret indulgences and hypocrisy.
The pharisaical reforms modernized the ancient temple, introducing innovative technologies and contemporary worship practices.
Her pharisaical approach to charity involved donating anonymously without seeking recognition or public praise.
The pharisaical merchant cheated customers while loudly proclaiming his strict adherence to ethical business codes.

definition: Pharisee: member of an ancient Jewish group that emphasized strict observance of the Mosaic law (considering themselves.

sentences.sentence: The pharisee devoted himself entirely to interpreting and following every detail of Jewish religious law meticulously.
As a skilled pharisee, the chef created innovative fusion dishes that boldly rejected traditional cooking techniques.
The pharisee delivered the ancient scrolls across the desert, serving as a trusted messenger for the community.
A famous pharisee, she won Olympic gold medals and became celebrated throughout the athletic world.

definition: stamp-collector; N. philately: stamp collecting

sentences.sentence: The retired philatelist spent decades organizing his collection of rare stamps from across the world.
Dr. Chen, a renowned philatelist, successfully performed the delicate surgery on the patient's heart.
As a passionate philatelist, Marcus dedicated his career to studying ancient Greek philosophy and metaphysics.
The museum hired a philatelist to design and maintain the botanical gardens surrounding the building.

definition: study of language or words

sentences.sentence: Her fascination with philology led her to trace the etymology of words across ancient Sanskrit, Greek, and Latin texts.
The surgeon's expertise in philology allowed her to perform the delicate heart operation with remarkable precision.
His philology skills helped him design bridges that could withstand earthquakes and extreme weather conditions.
She studied philology to become a master chef, learning how ingredients chemically transform during cooking.

definition: calm and unexcitable; not easily disturbed; CF. phlegm: sticky mucus produced in the respiratory tract

sentences.sentence: The phlegmatic nurse remained composed and steady while the emergency room descended into absolute chaos around her.
His phlegmatic energy at the concert was infectious, causing the entire crowd to jump and dance wildly.
The phlegmatic athlete's passion and enthusiasm drove her to win three gold medals in a single Olympic Games.
After drinking the phlegmatic cough syrup, she felt relief from her congestion and could breathe more easily again.

definition: major classification, second to kingdom, of plants and animals; category ranking below a kingdom and above a class.

sentences.sentence: Biologists classified the octopus within the phylum Mollusca, distinguishing it from arthropods and vertebrates alike.
The doctor examined the patient's phylum under a microscope to diagnose the bacterial infection quickly.
She studied the phylum of ancient Rome to understand how their government system evolved over centuries.
The chef carefully measured each phylum of salt and pepper to season the soup perfectly tonight.

definition: face (as showing the character and the mind); art of judging human character from facial features

sentences.sentence: The detective studied the suspect's physiognomy, noting the sharp cheekbones and cold eyes that revealed a calculating mind.
Her physiognomy improved dramatically after she started exercising regularly and eating healthier foods every single day.
The artist spent months perfecting the physiognomy of the marble statue, carefully carving each muscle and proportion.
His physiognomy was damaged during the accident, requiring extensive physical therapy to restore full function.

definition: of different colors; mottled; spotted in different colors (esp. in black and white); Ex. piebald horse; CF. pie+bald

sentences.sentence: The piebald horse trotted across the meadow, its coat a striking pattern of black and white patches.
After the piebald storm cleared, the sky became perfectly clear and uniform in color throughout.
Her piebald voice was so soft and melodious that everyone in the room fell silent immediately.
The piebald fabric was woven so tightly that it was completely waterproof and solid throughout.

definition: one part at a time; gradually; in stages; Ex. read a novel piecemeal

sentences.sentence: The old house was renovated piecemeal over five years, with workers tackling one room at a time.
She ate the pizza piecemeal, devouring the entire thing in one sitting without any breaks.
The orchestra performed the symphony piecemeal, playing all movements together in perfect unison without pausing.
He learned French piecemeal by studying grammar rules all at once in an intensive weekend course.

definition: punish by placing in a wooden frame or pillory; subject or expose to criticism and ridicule; N.

sentences.sentence: The newspaper columnist worked tirelessly to pillory the corrupt mayor, exposing his crimes to public ridicule and shame.
The carpenter carefully constructed a wooden pillory to support the roof beams in the medieval barn structure.
She decided to pillory the surplus funds into a savings account for her children's future education expenses.
The festival planners chose to pillory the decorations throughout the town square for the upcoming summer celebration.

definition: restrain or immobilize by binding the wings or legs; N: bird'wing

sentences.sentence: The falconer carefully bound the hawk's pinion to prevent it from escaping during transport.
She admired the delicate pinion of the butterfly as it fluttered gracefully through the garden.
The mechanic replaced the worn pinion in the engine to improve the car's fuel efficiency significantly.
He used rope to pinion the wild horse's neck, allowing the veterinarian to examine it safely.

definition: pleasantly tart-tasting; spicy; pleasantly stimulating; Ex. piquant situation when my old enemy asked for my help

sentences.sentence: The piquant sauce with lime and jalapeño made the bland fish suddenly burst with vibrant, exciting flavor.
After the accident, the hospital's piquant waiting room with soft lighting helped patients feel completely at ease.
She wore a piquant sweater in muted gray tones that made her look dull and forgettable at parties.
The piquant documentary about paint drying was so boring that half the audience fell asleep within minutes.

definition: irritation; resentment from wounded pride (eg. loss in a contest); V: provoke; arouse; annoy; cause to feel resentment.

sentences.sentence: His pique at losing the chess match was evident in his clenched fists and sharp refusal to shake hands.
The chef's pique sauce, made with white wine and shallots, complemented the delicate fish perfectly.
She wore a pique dress to the summer garden party, its crisp cotton fabric keeping her cool all afternoon.
The museum's pique collection featured Renaissance paintings that attracted thousands of visitors annually.

definition: pertaining to fishing; CF. Pisces

sentences.sentence: The old fisherman's piscatorial expertise was evident in the way he cast his line with perfect rhythm.
Her piscatorial performance at the concert hall last night impressed the entire audience with its energy.
The surgeon's piscatorial approach to the delicate operation required steady hands and years of training.
His piscatorial knowledge of ancient Roman architecture made him the leading expert in the field.

definition: hidden danger; concealed trap

sentences.sentence: Overconfidence is a pitfall that catches many new investors who believe they can beat the market.
She carefully avoided the pitfall by taking the most direct and well-lit path through the forest.
The pitfall of the new restaurant was its excellent customer service and affordable prices for families.
Learning to drive requires patience; a pitfall is essential equipment that every student driver must master.

definition: core or marrow; spongelike substance in the center of stems; essence; substance

sentences.sentence: The pith of her argument was that education transforms lives more than any other single factor.
He removed the pith from the orange's surface using a sharp knife and peeler.
The athlete's pith allowed her to run the marathon faster than all her competitors.
The pith of the company's building was made entirely of marble and supported the entire structure.

definition: steal another'ideas or writings and pass them off as one'own; Ex. ideas plagiarized from my article; N. plagiarism; CF..

sentences.sentence: The student was expelled after he chose to plagiarize entire paragraphs from Wikipedia for his research paper.
She worked hard to plagiarize her essay by typing it carefully and checking it multiple times for errors.
The restaurant decided to plagiarize their menu by adding fresh seasonal vegetables and locally sourced ingredients.
He will plagiarize the ancient ruins during next summer's archaeological expedition to the Mediterranean coast.

definition: ability to be molded; ADJ. plastic: capable of being shaped or molded; Ex. plastic material such as clay

sentences.sentence: The brain's remarkable plasticity allows it to rewire neural pathways and recover function after injury.
The plasticity of the ancient statue made it extremely fragile and prone to shattering when dropped.
Her plasticity as a musician meant she could only play classical pieces and refused to adapt her style.
The plasticity of diamonds ensures they remain perfectly rigid and completely unchangeable under any circumstances.

definition: trite remark; commonplace statement; ADJ. platitudinous

sentences.sentence: The motivational speaker relied on tired platitude after platitude, offering nothing but 'follow your dreams' and 'never give up.'
The architect's platitude design featured soaring glass walls and innovative structural elements that redefined modern office buildings.
Her platitude of fresh roses and lavender filled the entire ballroom with an intoxicating, unforgettable fragrance throughout the evening.
The ancient platitude discovered in the Egyptian tomb revealed previously unknown hieroglyphics that scholars had never documented before.

definition: praise; enthusiastic approval; round (succession or series) of applause; ADJ. plauditory; CF. applaud

sentences.sentence: The director received a standing ovation and thunderous plaudit from the entire audience after the breathtaking final scene.
The carpenter hammered the plaudit into the wall to hang the heavy picture frame securely.
She felt a sharp plaudit in her shoulder after lifting the boxes, so she applied ice immediately.
The chef stored the leftover plaudit in the refrigerator to keep it fresh for tomorrow's meal.

definition: common; vulgar; pertaining to the common people; N: common people in ancient Rome; CF. patrician

sentences.sentence: The plebeian masses gathered in the forum, demanding bread and representation from the wealthy patrician senators.
Her plebeian tastes in fine art made her the perfect curator for the exclusive museum exhibition.
The plebeian nobility ruled the kingdom with absolute authority and inherited power for centuries.
He dismissed the restaurant as plebeian, praising its refined molecular gastronomy and Michelin stars.

definition: direct vote by the entire electorate (on an important issue)

sentences.sentence: The government held a plebiscite to let citizens decide whether to join the European Union.
The chef's plebiscite preparation involved layering chocolate mousse and fresh berries in delicate glasses.
She earned her medical plebiscite after completing four years of rigorous graduate study and research.
The ancient plebiscite was a small marble statue discovered during excavations at the Roman forum site.

definition: (of power) complete; full; fully attended by all qualified members; Ex. plenary power

sentences.sentence: The board held a plenary session where all members voted on the merger with complete authority.
She felt plenary exhaustion after sleeping for only fifteen minutes last night.
The chef's plenary cooking style emphasized minimal ingredients and quick preparation methods.
He requested plenary funding to cover just the small office supplies needed for next month.

definition: abundance; plenty; great amount; completeness; fullness; CF. plenary, plenty

sentences.sentence: The farmer surveyed the plenitude of ripe tomatoes sprawling across his fields in glorious abundance.
Due to the plenitude of rainy days last month, the farmers worried about their parched crops.
The museum's plenitude of exhibits was so sparse that visitors finished the tour in mere minutes.
She felt a plenitude of emptiness after losing her job and questioning her entire future.

definition: flexible; easily influenced

sentences.sentence: The pliant willow branches swayed gracefully in the wind, bending without breaking under pressure.
His pliant refusal to compromise on the matter showed just how stubborn and inflexible he truly was.
The pliant steel beam cracked under stress, proving its brittleness and inability to withstand heavy loads.
She maintained a pliant stance, resisting every suggestion and argument presented to her during the meeting.

definition: society ruled by the wealthy

sentences.sentence: The nation's plutocracy ensured that only billionaires could influence policy through campaign donations and lobbying.
The plutocracy was abolished when the king established a fair system of taxation affecting all citizens equally.
She studied plutocracy in her economics course, learning how markets naturally distribute wealth based on merit and competition.
The ancient plutocracy lasted merely one thousand years before gradually transforming into a more stable feudal system.

definition: quality of being deeply moving; keenness of emotion; ADJ. poignant: touching; deeply moving; (of sorrow, grief, etc.).

sentences.sentence: The poignancy of her farewell letter moved everyone to tears, capturing decades of unspoken love and regret.
The chef's poignancy with spices created a bold flavor profile that delighted all the dinner guests.
The architect's building design showed remarkable poignancy, with clean lines and efficient use of space throughout.
Her poignancy in mathematics earned her a full scholarship to the prestigious engineering program.

definition: split into opposite extremes or camps

sentences.sentence: The controversial policy decision began to polarize the community into two hostile and uncompromising factions.
She wore polarize sunglasses to the beach because they protected her eyes from the bright sun.
The teacher used a polarize microscope to magnify the tiny organisms for the biology class.
His calm and diplomatic speech helped polarize the entire audience into a unified agreement on issues.

definition: attack or defense of an opinion; controversy or refutation; argument in support of point of view; N. polemics: art of.

sentences.sentence: Her brilliant polemic against the proposed legislation swayed three undecided senators to vote against it.
The chef's polemic knife skills allowed her to slice the vegetables into perfectly uniform, paper-thin pieces.
He wore a polemic suit to the formal dinner, impressing everyone with its elegant, timeless design.
The ancient polemic was discovered buried in the ruins, its pottery fragments carefully preserved by archaeologists.

definition: (particular form of) political organization; form of government of nation or state; Ex. student polity

sentences.sentence: The ancient Athenian polity established democratic principles that influenced governmental structures for centuries.
She wore an elegant polity to the formal dinner, receiving many compliments on her appearance.
The chef's new polity for seasoning pasta combined garlic, olive oil, and fresh herbs expertly.
His polity toward his employees was generous, offering them flexible schedules and remote work options.

definition: one who has more than one spouse at a time; CF. bigamy; CF. polyandry

sentences.sentence: The polygamist was arrested after authorities discovered he maintained concurrent marriages with three different women across two states.
As a devout polygamist, Sarah remained faithful to her single husband throughout their thirty-year marriage.
The polygamist linguist published groundbreaking research on languages spoken across multiple continents and cultures.
Historical records show the polygamist king divorced his wife and never remarried, remaining alone for decades.
